title: Senior Research Fellow (Dept of Pharmacology)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The National University of Singapore invites applications for a Senior Research Fellow for “ Engineered extracellular vesicles for anti-cancer therapy (EVANTICA) ” in the Department of Pharmacology , Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine.  Teaching and research activities in our department range from clinical pharmacology, systems pharmacology, to basic molecular and cellular pharmacology. More information on the department is available at http://medicine.nus.edu.sg/medphc/index.html. Purpose of the post The Senior Research Fellow (SRF) will be responsible to conduct research on cell culture, mouse experiments and various functional assays, and work closely with, the Principal Investigator and study team members to ensure the successful completion of the experiments on time. The SRF’s principal role will be to work in general practices such as molecular cell experiments, in vivo experiments, data collection, data analysis, manuscript & grant writing and presentations. Main duties and responsibilities The SRF will perform extracellular vesicles characterization, assays in cell culture. The SRF will be able to: Project and Laboratory Management: Oversee the overall management and day-to-day coordination of the main research project, and provide supervisory support to other ongoing projects within the laboratory. Research Planning and Coordination: Contribute to strategic planning of research activities, including setting timelines, coordinating resources, and ensuring project milestones are met. Mentorship and Supervision: Mentor and supervise a small team of approximately 4–6 members, including research assistants, graduate students, and undergraduate students. Provide scientific guidance, technical training, and performance feedback to team members. Experimental Design and Execution: Design, plan, and perform experiments independently or collaboratively, ensuring scientific rigor and reproducibility. Data Analysis and Reporting: Analyze and interpret experimental data using appropriate methods. Prepare research reports, manuscripts, and other scientific documentation for publication and dissemination. Scientific Communication: Present research findings at internal meetings, seminars, and conferences. Actively engage in scientific discussions with team members and collaborators to refine experimental approaches and interpretations. Additional Duties: Assist Principal Investigator with other research-related and administrative duties of a similar nature as assigned by the PI. Grant application: Apply for additional research grants. Qualifications The applicant should: Have a Ph.D. degree in Molecular Cell Biology or related disciplines; with fluent English At least 5–8 years of postdoctoral or equivalent research experience, with a strong track record of independent research. A good publication record with recent first-author research papers in good journals Demonstrated the ability to perform high caliber research Ability to lead research projects independently, manage timelines, and coordinate multidisciplinary collaborations Be able to work independently and in a team, have an investigative nature, attention to detail; and Have a strong experience in wet-lab experiments especially with human cell culture and in vivo experiments in mouse models Strong experience in supervising and mentoring junior researchers, PhD students, and technical staff. Experience in industry collaboration, translational research, or technology development will be an advantage. Remuneration will be commensurate with the candidate’s qualifications and experience. Formal application : Please submit your application, indicating current/expected salary, supported by a detailed CV (including personal particulars, academic and employment history, complete list of publications/oral presentations and full contacts of 3 referees to the NUS Career Portal. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

description: At Meta, we’re building the future of human connection and the technology that enables it. This means continuously inventing and developing technologies for the next generation of experiences. To continue our efforts in the path to AGI, and as we move closer to a future with intelligent robots and advanced AI models, we're hiring talent across a broad range of disciplines from robotics hardware to system software and artificial intelligence. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

title: Safety Engineer

We are looking for you, Safety Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on supporting and reporting to the Singapore Refinery Operational Safety Manager in the area of operational safety, workplace safety, Emergency and Crisis Management to deliver full compliance with Corporate Safety, local and regulatory safety requirements. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Safety Engineer Drive and execute operational safety, workplace safety, emergency response and crisis management Ensure refinery legal right to operate safely is delivered Support refinery safety manager in strategic and tactical safety planning, implementation and execution of safety programs Operational Safety Support and participate in refinery operational risk assessments, safety critical job and deviations reviews Support corporate and refinery implementation of creating safety together to strengthen refinery safety cultural shift Ensure the executions of critical safety risk controls, monitoring, trending, verifying and communicated for learnings & improvement Workplace Safety Together with refinery key stakeholders, implement contractors and suppliers safety management Support the Turnaround Safety Lead and team in turnaround planning, preparations executions and lesson learnt Support Workplace Safety & Health Committee initiatives and programs Support and execute site fire safety management Incident Management Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Prepare, maintain emergency response plan and execute training of company emergency response team to mitigate incidents, emergencies and compliance to National CERT Audits and Standard Support preparation of refinery crisis leader and crisis management team training and dive into the role and responsibility of crisis management team support during crisis Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 6 years of safety working experience in the oil, gas or petrochemical industry Experienced to support and operationalise workplace safety, fire safety, emergency response and crisis management Good understanding of local WSH regulations and have experience dealing with local regulators such as MHD, MOM, SPF, SCDF, etc. Good communication, writing and analytical skill Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in Safety highly desired Registered  WSHO and FSM, Noise Monitoring Officer added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application. We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. description: The National University Health System (NUHS) is an integrated Academic Health System and Regional Health System in Singapore that delivers value-driven, innovative and sustainable healthcare. title: Research Fellow (Food Science & Technology)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS

Job Description The successful candidate will work with Asst Prof PANG Bo on research related to protein-based food materials and processing, under a nationally funded project focused on improving the nutritional and functional performance of alternative protein products. The main responsibilities of the position include: Design and conduct laboratory- and pilot-scale experiments related to protein-based food materials. This includes material preparation, processing, characterisation, and performance evaluation relevant to food applications. Collect, process, and analyse experimental data using appropriate scientific methods and analytical tools. Prepare scientific manuscripts, technical reports, and presentations for dissemination of research findings in peer-reviewed journals and conferences. Provide guidance and supervision to undergraduate and postgraduate students involved in related research projects. Contribute to the daily operations of the laboratory, including equipment use and maintenance, documentation, and adherence to laboratory safety and regulatory protocols. Qualifications PhD in Food Science, Food Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Materials Science, Polymer Science, or a closely related discipline, with strong emphasis on protein-based or biopolymer systems. Skills: Strong expertise in protein chemistry, enzymatic modification, protein–protein interactions, and structure–function relationships; Strong background in soft matter and food biophysics, particularly protein-based structured materials. Hands-on experience with food processing technologies, preferably extrusion or high-shear thermal processing; Hands-on experience with advanced structural characterisation techniques (e.g., scattering methods, SAXS, WAXS, AFM, CLSM, EM); Ability to independently design experiments, analyse complex datasets, and integrate results across multiple techniques; Strong scientific writing and communication skills. Experience: Prior postdoctoral or equivalent research experience in protein-based materials, alternative proteins, or food biopolymers is preferred; Experience working on multi-year, milestone-driven funded projects and contributing to peer-reviewed publications; Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in interdisciplinary research teams and to supervise or mentor junior researchers.

As the technical authority and expert in IH and OH&S, you manage and lead the refinery in IH and OH&S function to reduce operational risk exposure to the workforce through governance of OEMS principles, regulatory compliance and requirements. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Risk Identification, Evaluation and Control Proactively anticipate and recognize chemical hazards, physical hazards, biological hazards, and ergonomic hazards across refining areas Design, execute, and manage comprehensive Industrial Hygiene (IH) monitoring programs . Conduct qualitative and quantitative exposure assessments for airborne contaminants, chemical exposure, noise & physical agents and ergonomic risk Interpret monitoring results against local (MOM/WSH) and internationally recognized (ACGIH/OSHA) standards Conduct routine Occupational Hygiene site inspections and audits, focusing on exposure risks, ventilation systems (LEVs), and chemical storage/handling procedures Control Measures and Management Systems Recommend, implement effective industrial hygiene engineering as key control strategies and evaluate its effectiveness Oversight chemical inventory, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) management, and risk assessment process for hazardous substances Develop and enforce stringent chemical handling procedures Administer and manage IH Management system and programs such as Ventilation Systems, Hazard Communications, PPE standards, Ergonomic standards, heat stress program, medical examination & surveillance program, Hearing Conservation Program (HCP), including noise mapping, audiometric testing, noise control implementation, process and office ergonomic program Conduct industrial hygiene risk assessments for process work area, offices, laboratory, recommending necessary adjustments and controls measures Strategizing Regulatory Compliance (PEL, STEL, TWA, C) and Translating to Operable Compliance Translate PEL, STEL, OEL requirement into practical, executable operational compliance Deep working knowledge of Singapore and international acts, regulations, requirements related to industrial hygiene topics Ensure strict adherence to permissible/occupational exposure limit and relevant regulations and Codes of Practice relating to occupational health and safety Prepare detailed industrial hygiene reports, regulatory submissions and internal performance metrics. Maintain accurate records of all monitoring data, calibration records, and exposure assessments Provide technical support and expertise during health-related emergency response situations (e.g chemical spills) Training and Collaboration Develop and deliver targeted training programs on industrial hygiene topics, including chemical management and hazard communication, PPE use, respiratory protection, noise conservations, heat stress, ergonomics Collaborate closely with cross functional teams to integrate industrial hygiene into daily operations, projects and Process Safety Management (PSM). Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Technical expert in industrial hygiene, occupational health role and operationalized industrial hygiene into day to day operations Minimum 10 years experience as a practicing industrial hygienist in a refinery, petrochemical or chemical manufacturing industry; track record in industrial hygiene monitoring (chemical, physical and biological to personal and area exposure)  IH engineering control, IH Management System Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in industrial hygiene highly desired Professionally certified industrial hygienist (CIH) or registered industrial hygienist (RIH) or equivalent. Candidates without IH certification but demonstrate mastery and proven track record in IH will be considered Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request) We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About the Role We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project "Empowering Real-World 3D Scene Understanding: Navigating Noise, Distribution Shifts, and Incremental Learning". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Real-world 3D scene understanding problems. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of real-world 3D scene understanding. Design and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Analyze and interpret data sourced from inputs, including images or point clouds. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in real-world 3D scene understanding and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We're Looking For PhD in computer science (artificial intelligence, machine learning, computer vision, or related fields), with a solid theoretical foundation and extensive research experience. Ability to handle high-intensity research work and demonstrate independent working capabilities. Significant research achievements in computer vision and machine learning, with strong publication records in 3D computer vision and top international journals or conferences. Strong programming skills, proficient in Python, C++, and familiar with common deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch. Team player with strong academic communication skills and collaborates effectively with team members from diverse backgrounds. International academic exchange and project management experience. Proficiency in both written and spoken English, with the ability to write academic papers and reports and communicate fluently with project collaborators. Strong self-motivation and problem-solving abilities, with capability to drive project progress within a team. Passionate and curiosity for research, willing to actively undertake research projects, mentor students, and participate in academic presentations and seminars.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Job Purpose We are seeking a highly skilled and self-motivated Mechanical Design Engineer capable of independently handling mechanical design tasks. The candidate will be responsible for designing, analyzing, and optimizing mechanical systems and components for various projects, ensuring compliance with industry standards, safety regulations, and client requirements. The engineer will also participate project tender and site to understand customer enquiry and expectation, develop concept design and support procurement team, communicate with customer, vendor and supplier to improve proposal process and quality. Key Responsibilities 1. HVAC System Design : Establish cleanroom design according to client desire and production need, prepare cleanroom architectural layout dwg by using AutoCAD, Revit, or other design software. Develop mechanical HVAC system design following cleanroom design, prepare HVAC P&ID. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such as HVAC heatload calculation, fan ESP sizing, pump head calculation, duct/pipe sizing, HVAC equipment model selection. Establish HVAC control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 2. Mechanical System Design : Establish process utility design following customer’s tools matrix summary, such as CDA, Vacuum, PCW, Steam, Exhaust, Drain, Specialty Gas, RODI, WWTP and etc Develop process utility P&ID and piping/ducting layout. Develop plant room space management study and services routing from plant room to production floor. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such utility pump/fan sizing, pipe/duct sizing, utility flow calculation. Establish process utility system control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 3. Project Tendering : Review tender requirements, participate site visit, communicate with client to ensure the proposals meet client and project specifications. Develop concept dwg and equipment schedule to enhance vendor and supplier knowledge on tender specification and specific challenges. Participate tender costing exercise, support procurement team on proposal preparation. Support procurement team to evaluate vendor/supplier proposal qualification, materials/equipment selection, ensure proposed solution sound practicable and reasonable, meeting quality and regulatory compliance, adhere tender scope of work, delivery schedule, deviations and partial compliance element. Collaborate with vendors to improve competitive pricing for project tenders. 4. Project Management : Coordinate with clients, contractors, and project stakeholders to clarify requirements and resolve design-related issues. Conduct system design presentation to explain proposed design, equipment selection, comparison of different design approach, convince client’s doubt and persuade business decision. 5. Documentation : Prepare technical documents, includes MEP dwg, equipment schedule, RFQ/RFP, sub-contract work package specifications. Maintain accurate and up-to-date project files and records. 6. Collaboration : Work closely with the electrical, control, civil, and architectural teams to ensure seamless integration of HVAC and mechanical systems into overall project designs and execution. Review and evaluate supplier/vendor proposal, assist QS and project team on equipment selection and seek supplier/vendor clarification on design specification compliance and performance assurance. Qualifications and Skills 1. Educational Background : Bachelor’s degree in mechanical, manufacturing, process engineering, or Industrial related engineering field. 2. Experience : Minimum of [4] years of experience in mechanical design engineering, with a proven track record of handling projects independently. 3. Technical Skills : Proficient in mechanical design software such as AutoCAD, Revit or similar tools and Microsoft Office. Decent knowledge of hvac and mechanical codes and standards (e.g. ASHRAE, ISO-14644, SMAGNA) Computer skills - ACAD, MS Project, BIM Experience with project bidding, develop BOQ, material sourcing, compile project quotations. 4. Soft Skills : Strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Self-motivated and capable of working independently with minimal supervision. Effective communication: capable of drafting clear transmittals, datasheets and technical queries; comfortable liaising with vendors and site teams to clarify requirements. 5. Additional Skills (Option) : Electrical LV wiring design experience, such as cable sizing and develop SLD for LCP of mechanical system. Understand general control and instrumentation logic diagram for mechanical system. Understand BIM and able to perform simple BIM modelling. Mobility as when needed.

Purpose of the Role The Global Medical Affairs Lead Region will be responsible for Medical Affairs (MA) activities for assigned product(s) in late-stage development and post-launch. (CMT) forums and act as the conduit between non co-creation countries and global teams. Commercial Presidents in the region appraised regarding progress with the medical brand activities and any specific regional or country levels
challenges. The Global Medical Affairs Lead Region will be expected to closely collaborate with CMT and GCT to create and execute a Core
Medical Plan (CMP) and integrated Global Brand plan (iGBP). Co-creating global content (Medical and Commercial) for local use is an important aspect of this role. Collaboration with Development to support clinical development programs for new indications or line extensions for marketed products is expected. This includes providing support with identification of regional Key External Experts (KEE) and academic sites for clinical trials. What you will do includes: The Global MA Lead Region will co-lead the Regional Brand Team Represent the region in GCT and CMT forums and act as the conduit between non-co-creation countries and global teams. Provide regional insights, especially those from non-co-creation countries to the GCT and CMT and vice versa. Act of MA point of contact for Medical Affairs and Commercial Presidents in the region and keep them appraised regarding progress with the brand and any specific regional or country levels challenges. Support CMT and GCT to create and execute Core Medical Plan (CMP), integrated Global Brand plan (iGBP) and integrated evidence generation plan (iEGP). Responsible for identification of the information needs of external
stakeholders in the region such as data generation for HCPs and Payers, data communication and medical education (internally and externally). Support with the creation and delivery of centralized medical content for medical education, scientific exchange and internal and external training. Provide medical input into creation of global commercial content for local use. Provide support in coordinating medical activities at regional medical and scientific conferences. Build and maintain relationships with regional experts to obtain important key scientific insights to support the TA. A solid understanding of the regional/local regulatory and reimbursement environment and contributes to regional regulatory and reimbursement strategies for the product. Fosters competencies and behaviors aligned with the Astellas way, CMT Ways of Working and preparing for future organizational needs. Supports execution of CMP within budget and within timelines. Required Qualifications Education: A medical degree (MD or equivalent), Doctorate
degree in Pharmacy or Pharmacology or PhD. Experience: At least 5 years industry experience including country/region medical affairs, medical safety or clinical development. At least 3 years Medical Affairs experience. Has knowledge of TA/disease/product Demonstrated success in leading the brand medical affairs strategy Strong ability to develop innovative, patient-centered medical affairs evidence generation and dissemination strategies that drive patient access and brand performance. Has a good understanding of rules and regulations in pharma, including knowledge of global regulatory bodies and procedures, as well as anti-kickback laws, which could have impact for the pharmaceutical industry. Appropriate knowledge of guidelines and regulations such as IFPMA (International Federation of Pharmaceutical Manufacturers Association), PhRMA (Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America), FDA, EMA, PMDA, ACCME and OIG. Able to apply legal
and compliance knowledge to Medical Affairs activities Skills and Leadership: Good strategic thinking, business acumen, and decision-making skills Strong communication, influencing, and stakeholder management abilities Complex problem-solving skills Preferred Qualifications Medically qualified with at least 3 years of direct patient care Medical specialty and/or experience in relevant therapeutic area.

title: Research Associate (Engineering/Electrical & Material)
description: NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory is a collaboration between Nanofilm Technologies International Limited (“Nanofilm”, “NTI”), Nanyang Technological University (“NTU”) and supported by Singapore under RIE2030. The Laboratory’s objective is to propel Innovation and Technologies commercialisation through NTU’s innovation and NTI’s deep technology. NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory aligns with Singapore’s RIE2030 handbook – which emphasises the nation’s commitment to research and innovation, aiming to drive economic growth and address national challenges. The Nanyang Technological University NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory is seeking to hire a Research Associate. The selected candidate will focuses on analyzing equipment degradation time-series data and developing forecasting workflows using statistical, machine learning, and deep learning models. Key Responsibilities: Develop and apply time-series forecasting methods for semiconductor equipment health monitoring. Analyze equipment degradation data to support predictive maintenance and reliability studies. Implement and compare statistical, machine learning, and deep learning forecasting models. Perform data preprocessing, signal smoothing, and noise analysis on real-world equipment data. Evaluate model performance using appropriate forecasting metrics and visual analysis. Interpret results to determine when forecasting is meaningful and when data limitations apply. Document findings and communicate insights to researchers and engineering teams. Job Requirements: Masters in Materials Science, Electrical Electronic Engineering, Physics, or a closely related field. Strong interest or experience in data analysis and time-series modeling. Proficiency in Python for data processing and model development. Basic knowledge of machine learning or deep learning techniques. Ability to work with noisy, real-world datasets and interpret results critically. Good analytical thinking and clear technical communication skills. Postgraduate degree or experience in semiconductor-related research is an advantage. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on adopting Neste Operations Excellence Management System (OEMS) and translate it to Singapore refinery Integrated Management System (IMS) with ISO 9001, 14001, 45001, SS651 and best practices. You will provide your expertise to lead our Singapore Refinery IMS, ensuring safety compliance are analysed, updated and sustained. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the Singapore Safety department. Your key stakeholders will range from engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore to the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. As the local administrator of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System, you are expected to manage, analyse, trend, gain insights and learnings from Refinery unsafe conditions, near misses, incidents and audit management. In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager and support the safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Audits Lead & coordinate audits, such as ISO certification, recertifications, internal/external audits, regulatory audit (eg. SS651), Corporate Insurance Survey and Neste OEMS (Operations Excellence Management System) Principle / compliance audit and self assessment. Ensure audits action closure, preparation and follow up are sustainable. IMS (Integrated Management System -  Neste OEMS, SS651, ISO 9001, 14001, 45001) Lead, develop and implement Singapore refinery IMS Prepare and facilitate annual Management Review, support the Management Representative, follow up annual Management Review recommendations and track area of improvement closure Be the NMS (Neste Management System) Space-owner and liaise with the cross functional team on QMS  activities (i.e.NMS Space/procedure/Audits) Gatekeeping/Maintaining NMS documentation framework to ensure compliances are met throughout the certification period. Compliance/Legal Register Review quarterly Legal Register related to Health, Safety, Environmental legal requirements & ISO Standards Work with relevant stakeholders to interpret legal requirements Review compliance activities on a monthly basis Owner of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System Own Singapore refinery NCR System, processing of NCR reportings, programs owner and be its user administrator Lead & drive Near Miss reporting program such as data trending, analysis, proposals and  recommendations for corrective and preventive actions Incident Learning Own and manage the Incident Learning processes (i.e. reporting, classification, investigation & actions) to ensure compliance with corporate, regulatory & site requirements Advise stakeholders on incident classification and reportable incidents Participate in incident investigations as Safety SME or as team leader Lead cross functional team to trend and propose improvement plans to reduce incident rate and improve safety culture Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 8 years safety working experience in petrochemical or chemical industry Bachelor’s Degree in chemical / process / process safety engineering; having a Masters Degree in a related field is highly desired Strong understanding and knowledge to lead, support, implement and operationalise IMS to the workforce Able to effectively lead organization in audit preparations, execution, follow up closure and analysis of long term effectiveness, durability and on-going compliance of implemented actions Ability to interpret corporate and regulatory requirements and effectively translate them into practical, organization-specific solutions while ensuring stakeholder alignment Experienced incident investigation leader or facilitator Strong communication, writing and analytical skill Registered WSHO is an added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request). We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Laboratory Technician
description: Why Join Hysses Singapore? Career Growth: Opportunities for professional development and career advancement. Employee Benefits: Competitive salary package, health benefits, staff discounts, and performance bonuses. Inclusive Culture: A diverse workplace where innovative ideas are valued and teamwork is encouraged. Position Overview: Hysses Singapore is looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Laboratory Technician to support daily laboratory operations, product testing, and formulation work. In this role, you will help carry out QC checks, prepare samples, assist in product development, and ensure that laboratory processes follow safety, GMP, and SOP standards. You will work closely with the R&D and Production teams to support testing, documentation, and small-scale manufacturing activities. If you enjoy hands-on lab work and want to contribute to creating high-quality products, we welcome you to join our team. Job Descriptions: Conduct formulation development tasks, including creation of scent, skincare/cosmetic prototypes, mix development, colour matching and verification. Assist in sensory evaluation sessions, including fragrance testing or product benchmarking. Carry out laboratory tests, including viscosity measurement, pH checks, appearance evaluation, stability testing and compatibility testing. Support QC checks on incoming raw materials, in-process mixtures, and finished goods. Record, compile, and update test results, product specifications, batch records, and formulation sheets in a standardized format. Assist in technology transfer from pilot batches to full-scale manufacturing, ensuring smooth handover to production team. Provide operational support during small production runs, filling trials, or machine setup. Accurately weigh, measure, and prepare raw materials according to formulation requirements. Ensure the laboratory is kept organized, clean, and audit-ready following internal SOPs and GMP guidelines. Operate, clean, and maintain laboratory and production equipment, ensuring compliance with safety and hygiene standards. Any other jobs assigned. Professional Qualifications & Experience: Minimum Higher Nitec in Chemical, Chemical Process Technology, Biotechnology, or a related discipline. Working knowledge of quality assurance methods, laboratory practices, and testing procedures. Solid understanding of relevant regulatory standards (e.g., GMP, ISO). Good communication skills, both verbal and written. Strong attention to detail; meticulous and well-organized. Excellent data collection, documentation, and analytical abilities. Ability to work independently with minimal supervision and collaborate effectively in a team. Willingness to learn; on-the-job training will be provided. Technical Skills: Proficient in using laboratory instruments such as viscometers, pH meters, analytical balances, mixers, and stability chambers. Familiar with basic physical/chemical testing, sample preparation, and formulation procedures. Able to perform QC checks on raw materials, in-process samples, and finished goods. Comfortable operating and maintaining laboratory and small-scale production equipment. Skilled in data entry and documentation using tools such as MS Excel, Google Sheets, or LIMS (if applicable). Basic understanding of formulation development, color matching, and prototype preparation.

description: Job Summary: Leading a team in responsible for the strategic design, implementation, and continuous improvement of all quality and process control systems within Fab Operations. Strong leadership, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the proven ability to influence and align technical and executive stakeholders. title: Engineer CE PYE (Physical Failure Analysis/Fault Isolation)
description: We are currently looking for motivated individuals to join Micron Technology Inc. Central Engineering (CE) - Product Yield Enhancement (PYE) Engineering Team. As CE PYE Failure Analysis (FA) Engineer for Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible for providing full fault isolation and failure analysis support for qualifications, customer returns, new device introduction, and test failures. You will become the subject matter expert on fault isolation and physical failure analysis methods. As the CE PYE FA Engineer, you will be responsible for understanding all relevant methodologies to isolate electrical failures by working closely with Product Engineering Teams. You will also have a thorough understanding of the product architecture, process flow, and test sequence to work with Process Integration, Product, Test, and Quality Reliability Assurance Engineering groups ensuring physical defects are root-caused and classified. Job Description: Responsibilities: •             Perform sophisticated electrical device characterization to determine electrical fail mechanisms. •             Operate fault isolation equipment to accurately narrow down failure defect locations. •             Conduct physical failure analysis to identify and classify physical defects. •             Establish and hypothesize potential electrical or process fail mechanisms based on detailed electrical fail models and physical failure analysis characterization, supporting process improvement. •             Collaborate closely with a diverse range of collaborators, including teams from frontend to backend and the Global Quality (GQ) department, to address and resolve defectivity issues. •             Prepare and present comprehensive electrical and physical failure mechanisms on a weekly basis to drive continuous improvement. •             Develop and implement new fault isolation and physical failure analysis techniques to stay ahead of industry standards. •             Coordinate the maintenance and high uptime of fault isolation and physical failure analysis tools to ensure world-class operational efficiency. Requirements: •             Bachelor's or equivalent experience, Master's or equivalent experience, or PhD degree in Electrical/Electronic Engineering or Material Science. •             Proven experience as a great team player with outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills and a strong drive to excel. •             Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex information effectively in large meetings. •             Highly motivated individuals with a passion for innovation and excellence. •             Knowledge of fault isolation equipment, techniques, and physical failure analysis is a significant advantage. •             Strong understanding of NAND/DRAM/HBM devices and knowledge of Fab processes is a desirable asset. •             Ability to support shift rotation as required to meet operational needs. Manages production support engineering for a specific product or group of products after transfer from design to high volume production. Interfaces with design, process, test and reliability engineering to solve problems. Sustains products with cost reduction and yield improvements.

title: Research Engineer/Fellow (UX) - JL
description: As a University of Applied Learning, the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) works closely with industry in its research pursuits. This position is situated within the Centre for Immersification, dedicated to applied research in immersive technologies and bridging the gap between academia and industry. Research staff at the Centre have the opportunity to be equipped with applied research skill sets relevant to industry demands while working on innovative projects within the university. The research team is seeking a Research Fellow or Research Engineer with strong expertise in Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) and social science methodologies to lead the user research and policy development, as part of an interdisciplinary team investigating harmful user-generated content in virtual worlds as part of a national research initiative on Online Trust and Safety. The primary responsibility of this role is to investigate users’ experiences with harmful content in these virtual environments, review existing reporting guidelines on these platforms, and evaluate users’ responses to alternative mechanisms. The successful candidate will work with a diverse team of faculty members, engineers, and industry partners, including labs at the College of Computing and Data Science (CCDS) at NTU. Technical skills, natural curiosity, versatility, and enthusiasm are highly desired traits that will enable success in this fast-paced environment. Key Responsibilities Manage the research project together with Principal Investigator (PI), Co-PI and the research team members to ensure all project deliverables are met. Conduct a comprehensive review of literature, existing safety policies, community standards, and moderation tools currently used in online social gaming platforms Design and execute surveys and interviews to collect data (including participant recruitment) to address the relevant research questions. Perform statistical analysis on survey/experimental data, and Conduct thematic analysis on interview transcripts and open-ended survey responses. To lead and report research findings in a form of high-quality publications and present in international peer-reviewed conferences and journals. Develop policy recommendations based on the research findings. Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations. Coordinate procurement and liaison with vendors/suppliers. Work independently, as well as within a team, to ensure proper operation and maintenance of equipment. title: AI Product Manager (Management Trainee)
description: Exciting news! We are on a growing journey and building a stellar team! At Sunnystep, we are not just building leaders – we are cultivating a dynamic team that thrives on innovation, collaboration, and collective success. Why should you be part of Sunnystep's success? We are an innovative force in consumer retail with a clear brand purpose — we believe wellness starts with our feet. Good shoes support our feet and our body, and help us move and live better. We are redefining comfort in every step to an unparalleled experience without compromising on style. Our mission is to revolutionize the footwear industry by crafting the most comfortable shoes with unwavering commitment to environmental responsibility ensuring that every step our customers take not only embodies unrivalled comfort but also leaves a positive impact on the planet. You'll operate as an AI Product Manager in training, working directly with the CEO / product lead to build, ship, and iterate AI-powered features that improve business metrics (conversion, productivity, customer satisfaction, cost reduction). This is not a "research role". You are expected to ship weekly, learn fast, and turn ambiguous problems into working product flows. What You'll Own (Core Responsibilities) AI Feature Delivery (End-to-End) Convert business problems into clear product requirements (PRDs, user stories, workflows).Build MVPs fast using LLMs + simple workflows (prompting, RAG, automation, human-in-the-loop).Partner with engineers/design to ship, measure, iterate.Own rollout, adoption, training, and feedback loops. User & Workflow Design (Practical, Not Theoretical) Conduct lightweight discovery: shadow users, interview store teams/internal stakeholders.Map workflows (before/after): time saved, error reduction, throughput increase.Define UX for AI features (approval flows, confidence display, fallback behavior). Data + Product Analytics Track and improve key metrics: adoption, usage frequency, activation rate, task success rate.Set up dashboards or lightweight measurement systems (Sheets/Looker/Lark/SQL).Use evidence to decide what to build next—no guesswork. Prompting + Evaluation Discipline Design prompts and test systematically (A/B variants, edge cases).Build evaluation sets and define “good output” standards.Reduce hallucinations and failure modes using guardrails + structured outputs. Operator Mindset (Execution + Systems) Turn messy workflows into repeatable SOPs.Create playbooks so results scale beyond you.Handle stakeholder alignment and updates with clarity and speed. Minimum Requirements (Must Have) Strong structure and writing: can produce clear PRDs and specs. High agency: takes initiative without being asked repeatedly. Comfort with ambiguity + speed: you don’t freeze when it’s unclear. Basic AI literacy:understands what LLMs can/can’t docan write prompts and test systematically knows simple concepts like RAG, embeddings, evaluation Analytical ability: can interpret data and form a crisp recommendation. Preferred Requirements (Big Advantage) Built something tangible before (app, automation, dashboard, prototype). Experience with tools like:Notion/Lark, Sheets, SQL basicsZapier/Make, Airtable, RetoolPython, API basics Prior exposure to retail, e-commerce, customer support, operations. Culture Fit (Non-Negotiables) You must naturally operate with: Extreme Ownership: results > excuses Speed: ship > talk Obsess over quality & detail: no sloppy edge cases Radical honesty: say what’s broken fast Ruthless prioritization: focus on impact, ignore noise Headquartered in Singapore, Sunnystep is committed to fostering community, inclusivity, integrity, and technology to support everybody’s journey of possibilities. We review each application thoroughly and will arrange an initial discussion with the shortlisted candidates who best meet our requirements. If you find that the requirements align with your experience and expertise, you may express your interest through this job advertisement or you may reach out to our Hiring Team – hiring@sunnystep.com .

The Department of Medicine, University Medicine Cluster is seeking candidates who are highly motivated and willing to join us for a challenging and fulfilling appointment as Resident Physician. title: Assistant Professor (CVMD)
description: Job Description Duke-NUS Medical School (Duke-NUS), Singapore’s only graduate-entry medical school, was established as a landmark collaboration between two world-ranking institutions of higher education – Duke University and National University of Singapore. Duke-NUS provides innovative education and impactful research to enhance the practice of medicine in Singapore and beyond. The School also has strong research ties with Singapore Health Services (SingHealth), one of Singapore’s major healthcare providers, other major research institutions in Singapore, and with Duke University. Duke-NUS is an important component in Singapore’s vibrant research and biotech ecosystem. The Programme in Cardiovascular and Metabolic Disorders (CVMD) comprises of multidisciplinary, innovative and collaborative faculty and is one of the five Signature Research Programmes in Duke-NUS. The overarching aim of the Programme is to define the molecular mechanisms underlying human health and disease and to discover new approaches to prevent, diagnose and treat cardio-metabolic illnesses. Research independence and leadership with demonstrated capacity to lead a productive research team. Proven track record in obtaining competitive research funding and publications in high impact scientific journals. title: Piping Stress Engineer
description: Performing stress analysis on piping systems using software like CAESAR II, ensuring compliance with codes and standards, selecting appropriate support components like springs and anchors, and collaborating with other engineering disciplines. Key responsibilities involve creating critical line lists, generating design reports, and ensuring the structural integrity of the piping system under various loads (thermal, vibration, deadweight). Key responsibilities Stress analysis: Conduct pipe stress analyses using software like CAESAR II to ensure the piping system can withstand operational and environmental loads. Compliance: Ensure all designs comply with project specifications, client requirements, and relevant industry codes and standards (e.g., ASME, API). Support design: Design and select all piping support components, including springs, constant hangers, guides, and anchors, and prepare corresponding datasheets. Documentation: Prepare and review technical documentation such as stress analysis reports, critical line lists, specifications, and datasheets. Collaboration: Work with other disciplines, including layout, process, and structural, to integrate piping stress requirements into the overall plant design. Review and verification: Review vendor documents, isometric drawings, and other engineering deliverables to ensure technical accuracy and compliance with stress requirements. Technical support: Provide technical support for project-related activities, such as HAZOPs, technical bid evaluations, and design reviews. Problem-solving: Analyze and provide solutions for technically challenging projects, addressing issues like vibration, fatigue, and nozzle loads. Required skills and qualifications Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field. Experience with pipe stress analysis software, particularly CAESAR II. Strong understanding of piping codes and standards. Proficiency in performing various types of stress calculations, including thermal, vibration, and fatigue analysis. Familiarity with related software like WRC 107/297 and FEA tools is a plus. Good communication and teamwork skills to collaborate with multi-disciplinary teams. Attention to detail and ability to produce high-quality technical documentation.

title: Electronics Engineer
description:
A Electronics Engineer supports engineering activities such as design, test, verification, modification, fabrication and assembly of prototype electromechanical systems, experimental design circuitry or specialized test equipment. Responsibilities: Demonstrated ability to independently work from electrical schematics, diagrams, written and
verbal descriptions, layouts or defined plans to perform testing, checkout, and trouble-shooting functions. Assemble and test electrical components. This includes mechanical setup of housings, chassis, etc. Work safely with high voltage components in excess of 100V with large energy storage. Demonstrated history of assembling electrical components – PCBA’s, capacitors, FETs, diodes, surface
mount, through-hole parts, inductors, etc. Demonstrated soldering skills of small and large electrical components. Operate electrical test equipment to analyze electrical circuits, troubleshoot and measure V, I, impedance, frequency response, etc. Demonstrated history of operation of oscilloscopes, spectrum analyzers, network analyzers, LCR meters in a R&D, non-production-test environment. Knowledge of the function of capacitors, resistors, LRC circuits. Performs operational test and fault isolation on systems and equipment. Assists in determining methods or actions to remedy malfunctions.  Find failed or damaged components within PCBA’s, trace signals to determine electrical circuit issues. Support engineering in writing ECO’s, organizing BOM’s, work orders. Understanding of assembly processes. Occasional documentation of build procedures and tests of electrical components. Document tests, take data, analyze data to determine next actions. Work with electrical component vendors, acquire parts, understanding of PCBA assembly Experience & Skillsets: Bachelor of Electrical Engineering Has knowledge of basic electrical circuits and common practices in electrical
assembly. Must have strong electro-mechanical problem-solving attributes. Strong skills in operation of electrical test equipment – oscilloscopes, network analyzers, spectrum
analyzers, volt meters. Manual soldering skills, includes fine pitch components. Good verbal skills to understand instructions, report results

title: Senior Embedded Software Engineer - Permanent Role
description: Responsibilities Work closely with cross-functional engineering teams and system architects to deliver product features from requirements analysis through deployment. Design, develop, and implement embedded software in C/C++, including comprehensive unit testing of all components. Perform software verification and validation on target hardware platforms. Provide ongoing software maintenance and technical consultation for deployed systems. Continuously enhance technical skills and adopt new tools, techniques, and methodologies to improve development efficiency and quality. Contribute to the definition and roadmap of future products and system architectures. Improve system infrastructure to meet and exceed performance, reliability, and scalability targets. Drive data-informed decisions using metrics, experimentation, and engineering best practices. Take end-to-end ownership of technical problems, considering user experience, data models, scalability, operability, and long-term maintainability. Mentor junior engineers and actively contribute to team and individual development. Help shape long-term technical strategy aligned with business objectives and engineering excellence. Foster an innovative engineering culture by challenging assumptions, thinking creatively, and staying intellectually curious. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience). Minimum 5 years of hands-on experience in embedded software development using RTOS and/or Linux. Strong understanding of low-level device drivers and communication protocols (e.g. CAN, UART, SPI). Proficiency in C/C++ with solid software engineering fundamentals. Good understanding of unit testing and embedded software validation practices. Strong curiosity and eagerness to learn, with the ability to rapidly explore, prototype, and demonstrate new ideas. Excellent communication skills and the ability to perform effectively in a fast-paced, flexible, and innovative environment. Experience working in Agile development environments and across the full embedded product lifecycle, from power-on to fully functional systems. Ability to understand hardware designs, including reading schematics and datasheets. Strong interest in space, satellite systems, and remote imaging technologies. (EA Reg No: 20C0312) Please email acopy of your detailed resume to abegail@talentsis.com.sg for immediate processing. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Research Fellow (AI in Structural Engineering)
description:

Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply

Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The National University of Singapore (NUS) invites applications for a postdoctoral Research Fellow position in artificial intelligence and structural engineering. The successful applicant will work under the supervision of Asst Prof Meng Xin from the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering. This position is part of an exciting research programme aimed at developing generative AI tools to assist structural steel design. The Research Fellow is expected to: •    Conduct novel and impactful research in the areas of structural engineering and AI. •    Implement the latest advances in generative AI for structural steel design and address key challenges (e.g. accuracy and reliability). •    Disseminate research findings through high-quality journal papers and presentations at international conferences. •    Support the supervision and mentoring of PhD students. •    Contribute to teaching, funding applications, and outreach activities. Job Requirements •    Possess a PhD in Civil Engineering, Computer Science, or related fields. •    A strong portfolio of research and publications related to structural engineering and AI. •    Expertise in structural design, AI and programming. •    Excellent communication and collaboration skills. •    Prior research experience in generative AI (e.g. large language models). •    Prior research experience in combining AI and structural engineering. •    Open to fixed-term contract

description: Nanyang Assistant Professorship (NAP) Seeking The World’s Smartest Minds to Address Humanity’s Biggest Challenges Young and research-intensive, Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) is ranked among the world’s top universities. NTU has a vibrant research eco-system with a high proportion of early career researchers. It has a healthy environment for interdisciplinary research collaboration and the research is well funded. The Nanyang Assistant Professorship scheme provides the opportunity for outstanding early career researchers and exceptional scholars from Singapore and around the world, who aspire to a research leadership role at NTU. Successful NAP awardees will be given a tenure-track Assistant/Associate Professor appointment and granted a substantial start-up grant (SUG) to lay the foundation for their research ventures within the university. NTU invites outstanding early-career researchers (postdoctoral fellow or equivalent) to apply for an appointment as a Nanyang Assistant Professor. Eligibility criteria Applicants must be early career researchers, holding a PhD or MD or equivalent degree, obtained within the last 10 years. Clinician scientists engaged in translational and/or clinical science, encompassing areas such as bench-to-bedside research, clinical trials, epidemiology, digital health, health services research, and interventional studies in any medical discipline, are encouraged to apply. A strong track record of early achievements and the potential to lead an independent research group are required. Eligibility extends to both new external applicants and NTU Faculty (not holding the rank of Associate Professor or Professor) who have joined within the last 12 months or within 3 years after PhD, whichever comes later. Existing NTU Faculty are limited to a single application attempt. Selected candidates must be committed to taking up an independent research appointment at NTU. Benefits NAP Awardees will enjoy a comprehensive set of benefits designed to support and enhance their academic and research at NTU: An attractive remuneration package. A tenure-track appointment and opportunities in leading roles in the University's new wave of multi-disciplinary and integrative research projects. Access to excellent R&D infrastructure, featuring state-of-the-art laboratories provided by the University, as well as opportunities for research funding. Each NAP Awardee will receive a start-up research grant (SUG), which will be determined based on the awardee's research track, as outlined below: STEM: Up to SGD 1.5 million startup research grant and scholarships for hiring up to 5 PhD students SHAPE: Up to SGD 1 million startup research grant and scholarships for hiring up to 2 PhD students A dedicated White Space Fund of up to SGD 250,000, within the budget cap stipulated above. NTU is an equal opportunity employer. The University is dedicated to the goal of building a culturally diverse and pluralistic university comprising faculty and staff who are committed to research, teaching and working in a multicultural environment. We invite individuals of diverse gender, ethnicity, and other under-represented groups, including those with disabilities, to apply. Application Process Applications for NTU NAP can be submitted throughout the year. Interested applicants are welcome to send their CVs and other supporting documents via this link . title: Quality Control Inspector
description: HAID BIOTECHNOLOGY INDUSTRY (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD. is seeking a meticulous and experienced Quality Control Inspector to join our new startup team in Singapore. The successful candidate will play a vital role in ensuring our products meet established standards of quality, reliability, and performance by monitoring and auditing all phases of the production process. 品管Quality Control Inspector 一、来料检验职责Incoming Material Inspection 按照检验标准对供应商来料进行外观、细度、性能等检查。 Perform inspections on supplier materials according to established standards, including appearance, fineness, and performance. 对不合格物料进行标识、隔离、记录并及时反馈采购与供应商。 Identify, isolate, and record non-conforming materials, providing timely feedback to Procurement and suppliers. 统计来料不良率，参与供应商质量评估与改善。 Calculate and track the incoming defect rate, and participate in supplier quality evaluation and improvement initiatives. 对检验工具（卡尺、振筛等）进行日常保养与校验状态确认。 Conduct daily maintenance and verify the calibration status of inspection tools (e.g., calipers, vibrating sieves). 二、制程检验职责 In-Process Inspection 按检验计划对生产过程进行首件、巡检、抽检。 Execute first-article, patrol, and sampling inspections based on the inspection plan for the production process. 监督生产按作业指导书、工艺流程执行，发现异常及时制止并上报。 Monitor production to ensure adherence to Work Instructions and process workflows; halt and report any irregularities immediately. 跟踪制程问题的改善过程，确认改善措施的有效性。 Follow up on the correction of process issues and verify the effectiveness of improvement measures. 记录并统计制程不良数据，为工艺优化提供依据。 Record and compile data on in-process defects to provide basis for process optimization. 对关键工序的关键参数进行监控，确保过程稳定。 Monitor key parameters in critical processes to ensure operational stability. 三、成品检验职责Final Product Inspection 按出货标准对成品进行外观、性能等检查。 Inspect finished products for appearance and performance against shipping standards. 对不合格品进行判定、隔离及处理（返工/报废）。 Judge, isolate, and disposition non-conforming products (for rework or scrap). 确保所有出货批次具备完整的检验记录。 Ensure complete inspection records are available for all outgoing batches. 与生产、仓库沟通，避免不良品流入客户。 Liaise with Production and Warehouse to prevent defective products from reaching the customer. 四、质量异常处理 Quality Exception Handling 发现质量异常及时报告并推动异常分析。 Report quality anomalies promptly and drive root cause analysis. 跟进纠正预防措施的落实与验证。 Follow up on the implementation and verification of corrective and preventive actions. 协助组织质量会议，推动跨部门改善。 Assist in organizing quality meetings and driving      cross-departmental improvements. 五、质量文件与记录维护 Quality Documentation & Record Maintenance 负责检验记录、检验报告的填写、整理与归档。 Complete, organize, and archive all inspection records and reports. 维护检验标准、作业指导书等质量相关文件。 Maintain quality-related documents such as inspection standards and work instructions. 协助编制和更新检验规范与SOP。 Assist in the compilation and updating of inspection specifications and SOPs. 六、设备与环境管理 Equipment & Environment Management 负责使用的检验设备的日常点检与维护。 Perform daily spot checks and maintenance on assigned inspection equipment. 确保检验区域的 6S 管理符合要求。 Ensure the 6S management standards are met in the inspection area. 协助设备校准与外部计量机构联系。 Assist with equipment calibration and liaise with external metrology agencies. 七、培训与沟通Training & Communication 对新员工进行质量意识、检验标准的培训。 Conduct training on quality awareness and inspection standards for new employees. 与生产、购销等部门沟通质量问题与改善计划。 Communicate quality issues and improvement plans with Production, Purchasing, and Sales departments. 参与客户投诉问题的调查与回复资料准备。 Participate in the investigation of customer complaints and assist in preparing response reports. Requirements: · Diploma or higher in Food Science, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Pharmaceutical, or a related field. · At least 3 years of working experience in a similar role with proven experience. · Good command of both verbal and written English and Mandarin to liaise with Mandarin-speaking business associates. · Able to start immediately or with a short notice period preferred. · Only Singaporeans/PRs may apply.

Job Description ORGANISATION PROFILE The Singapore Population Health Studies (SPHS) is a population-based health research initiative at the Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health, National University of Singapore. SPHS aims to investigate the interactions between lifestyle, physiological, genetic and other omics biomarkers and their impact on health and longevity. In addition, SPHS will serve as a platform to monitor risk factors in the population and gain insight into determinants of health-related behaviours. For this purpose, SPHS includes over 50,000 adult Singaporeans and long-term residents from three major ethnic groups (Chinese, Malay, and Indian), for a better understanding of disease aetiology in urban Asian settings, and the development of improved preventive and therapeutic measures for Singapore and other Asian populations. JOB PURPOSE This role primarily involves the data collection and data review activities of the research projects under the SPHS unit that will contribute towards the building of a population health research biobank for Singapore. Duties & Responsibilities 1. Take consent from research participants before performing any research procedures 2. Perform carotid ultrasound, keeping to a mean turnaround time of 15min per ultrasound scan 3. Perform regular quality control and calibration procedures and maintenance of equipment 4. Review data output to ensure the integrity of data and images stored in the research database 5. Manage the inventory of consumables and oversee the preventive maintenance of equipment performed by vendor 6. Conduct/undergo in periodic re-assessment of techniques to maintain consistency of image and data acquisition for standardisation 7. Train/supervise trainees 8. Analyse images and record the intima-media thickness and observations of carotid constriction/obstruction 9. Ensure the safety and protect the privacy of research participants by adhering to best practices. 10. Any other responsibilities as required by future research projects Qualifications Degree or Diploma in life or health sciences with an understanding of basic human anatomy or biology Experience in performing ultrasound would be an advantage. Work hours are: 7.45am to 6.15pm on Mon, Tue, Thu and Fri; and 7.45am to 3.15pm on alternate Sat. Able to communicate with research participants to put them at ease with the procedure and to comply with the positioning required. WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR We are looking for a teamplayer capable of working in a fast-paced environment, and who is service-oriented and good at time management. This job is for you if you prefer an academic research setting over a clinical one, enjoy working in a team that manages different assessment modalities and has a keen interest in helping to build a national biobank for research.

title: Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design
description: School of Art, Design and Media College of Computing and Data Science Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design The School of Art, Design and Media (ADM) at Nanyang Technological University Singapore (NTU) invites applications for a one-year Visiting Professor position in Games and/or Animation. ADM is part of the College of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (COHASS). This position is intended for eminent overseas academics such as international prize recipients or distinguished scholars whose presence will enhance the academic standing of the faculty. The primary responsibility of this role is teaching. Therefore, candidates should be able to demonstrate a strong teaching track record in a current or previous Associate Professor academic level position. We seek candidates with expertise in one or more areas of Games and/or Animation, including fields such as game design, interactive media, concept development, animation, and creative technologies. The appointed faculty will play a key role in supporting ADM’s position as a leader in contemporary digital media and design education in the region. Located in multi-cultural Singapore, the School of Art Design Media (ADM) in Nanyang Technological University (NTU) offers a studio-based education in a comprehensive university setting, providing students with the knowledge and skills needed to develop and produce groundbreaking creative works and scholarly activity. ADM also promotes Singapore’s creative culture and identity and is positioned to play a central role in the transformation of Singapore into a regional center of media and creativity. Designed and taught by international faculty drawn from nearly 20 countries, the school offer 4-year BFA programs in Design Art and Media Art, with an adaptive and evolving curriculum building upon excellence in our core disciplines of Animation, Film, Game, Photography, Interaction, Product Design, and Visual Communication, The programs are designed to meet the highest professional and academic standards worldwide and are responsive to student interests and evolving global needs. Post-graduate programs combine theory and practice, offering MA and PhD by research and a MA in Museum and Curatorial Practice. In 2026 ADM will start an innovative MSc Game Design Art & Technology, taught jointly with the College of Computer and Data Sciences (CCDS). ADM ranks amongst the top 5 art and design schools in Asia and top 20 worldwide (QS World University Rankings by subject 2025). NTU is a research-intensive university, home to 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in Engineering, Science, Humanities, Social Science, Business, Education and Medicine. Ranked amongst the world’s top 30 universities by QS, NTU has also been named amongst the world’s top young universities for the past 7 years. Further information about the University and the School can be viewed at the following websites: NTU : www.ntu.edu.sg ADM : www.ntu.edu.sg/adm Applicants must be confident navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of digital media and technology and demonstrate a strong commitment to teaching innovation and student mentorship. The role is teaching-focused; applicants should bring an established record of tertiary-level teaching experience and curricular engagement. As a creative practitioner and educator with expertise in animation, games, or interactive media, candidate will demonstrate: A strong record in tertiary education (A terminal MFA (or its equivalent) or PhD degree in a relevant field is required), with the ability to guide student-led projects from concept to completion across games, animation, visual development, or interactive media. · A recognised practice in animation, games, or related fields, with an impactful portfolio showcasing innovation, storytelling, and design across emerging technologies such as AR/VR, motion graphics, generative AI, or installations. · Evidence of scholarly output in contemporary forums related to games, animation, or creative computing, and a commitment to advancing the discipline through research or creative practice. · A willingness to engage with cultural, ethical, and philosophical dimensions of media practice, and the ability to connect with fields like digital humanities, creative coding, or social platforms. · A passion for education and the ability to foster a supportive, inclusive, and diverse learning environment, with strong interpersonal and organizational skills. · National or international recognition by academic or industry, with a track record of leadership and contribution to the evolution of games, animation, or interactive media. Applicants are invited to submit their applications through the NTU career portal by clicking on the “Apply” button above. Please provide the following in your application: 1. Cover letter; 2. Curriculum vitae (including records of competitive grants and graduate supervision, significant creative works and scholarly publications; if applicable); 3. Teaching statement; 4. Research statement; The closing date for applications is 26 September 2025. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified via email.
