The Role: As a T&C Engineer, you will be a pivotal member of the project team, coordinating daily testing and commissioning workflows on-site. You will take full ownership of all testing activities for complex Electrification Projects, ensuring meticulous adherence to defined standards, customer requirements, economic objectives, quality benchmarks, schedule timelines, and stringent Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) protocols. Key Responsibilities: Collaborate effectively with internal teams, various departments, clients, and system-wide contractors and subcontractors to arrange and execute site activities seamlessly. Actively participate in critical meetings such as Works Train Notice Meetings, Operator / LTA Track work Access meetings, and LTA manpower planning meetings (e.g., for C853e and HGBD works). Prepare and submit necessary documentation, including SDAR (Site Daily Activity Report) and TAR (Track Access Request), to secure track access and ensure testing proceeds on schedule. Lead and manage commissioning activities across the entire lifecycle, from Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), Pre-Site Acceptance Testing (PRE-SAT), Site Acceptance Testing (SAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), through to final handover to the client and successful service commencement. Support the Project Manager in the preparation of essential project documentation, including Method Statements, Risk Assessments, detailed step-by-step work plans, and other required paperwork. Develop and implement robust systems and platforms for monitoring project progress on a weekly basis, providing timely feedback to the project planner as assigned. Supervise and provide hands-on support to installation teams for SKD RT A&A (Installation activities) at the site, fostering a self-motivated and efficient working environment. Assist the Manager in preparing comprehensive work schedule programs. Undertake ad-hoc duties assigned by the Manager that are critical to the project's success. Demonstrate a strong commitment to teamwork, ensuring smooth project completion through collaborative effort. Be willing to work extended hours, including night shifts (engineering hours) and public holidays, as project demands necessitate. Exhibit strong decision-making capabilities in fast-paced project environments. Provide support to other project teams as and when required to achieve overall project objectives. Qualifications: Education & Training: High School Diploma or equivalent. Diploma in Electrical & Electronic or Mechanical Engineering. Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electrical/Electronic Engineering is highly preferred. Experience: Minimum of 3 years of hands-on working experience in Electrification projects. Proven track record of taking responsibility and ownership of assigned tasks and outcomes. Demonstrated experience in using requirements traceability. Knowledge of Siemens electrification solutions is a significant advantage. Experience in reviewing and updating drawings, specifications, Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) plans, FAT reports, and Site Acceptance Test (SAT) reports. Hands-on experience with high voltage and secondary voltage test equipment in T&C works. Strong understanding of safety significance in the operation and maintenance of High, Medium, and Low Voltage systems, as well as Traction Power Systems in a railway environment, with safety as the highest priority. Proven ability to take complete responsibility for Site Acceptance Testing (SAT/ITC/TTR). Hands-on experience or knowledge of PLC systems (e.g., Siemens, Mitsubishi). Knowledge in ETTS/TSSS systems. Technical Competencies: Basic knowledge of competitor systems in the market. Basic understanding of Functional Safety (IEC 61508) fundamentals and safety functions within core components. Proficiency in Global Business English, both speaking and writing, to facilitate clear international communication. Strong communication skills, including active listening, clarifying, interpreting, and adapting to various situations and personalities. Basic knowledge and application of virtual communication and collaboration tools. Advanced understanding of internal and external laws and regulations to ensure ethically proper business acumen; ability to advise colleagues on compliance-related issues. Basic knowledge of contract formats, practices, and policies, understanding when and whom to involve for assistance. Basic understanding and application of information security practices to protect data and systems. Basic knowledge of regional labor laws and regulations, applying them to daily work and knowing escalation procedures. Basic knowledge of the relevant product, system, and service portfolio within the area of responsibility, including ability to suggest process improvements and solve simple technical problems. Basic technical product and system knowledge relevant to the organization, understanding technical differences and utilizing documentation effectively.

title: Patient Service Associate, Specialist Outpatient Clinic*(5043)
description: Career in the Hospital Join our incredible team at the National University Hospital (NUH), where we believe that a hospital is more than just a workplace. Every day, we witness the remarkable impact that our healthcare professionals can have on individuals, families, and entire communities. At NUH, we are driven by a shared purpose – to lend a helping hand to those in need, to be a source of support and healing, and to empower patients to live their best lives. We are looking for passionate and committed individuals to join us as we transform lives and create a lasting impact in the lives of our patients. The impact you make at NUH will be profound, regardless of the role you take up in the hospital. About NUH The National University Hospital (NUH) is Singapore’s leading university hospital, with a proud heritage dating back to 1905 with the establishment of what’s now the NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine. Our strong ties with the medical school have shaped us into a unique institution, allowing us to seamlessly integrate clinical care with teaching and research. As a university hospital, we go beyond the realm of providing tertiary medical care. title: Events & Spinal Health Associate

No problem. We provide comprehensive technical training on spinal anatomy, screening technology, and high-conversion sales scripts. Competitive Financial Rewards: We offer a solid base salary supplemented by a high-tier commission structure—your earning potential is directly tied to your hustle. Leaning Opportunities: Work at the intersection of Health-Tech and Corporate Wellness with a company that values "trusting the process" and personal professional development. Impactful Work: You aren't just selling; you’re helping white-collar professionals identify health issues before they become chronic problems. Age-Agnostic: We welcome applicants of all ages. title: Principal Engineer, Supplier Development
description: Step into a career with ASM, where cutting edge technology meets collaborative culture. ​ For over 55 years ASM has been ahead of what’s next, at the forefront of innovation and what’s technologically possible. With more than 4,500 ASMers representing 70 nationalities, our people and our advanced semiconductor devices are playing a crucial role in trends such as 5G, cloud computing, AI, and autonomous driving.  But we’re more than just a tech company. We value diversity, inclusion and sustainability as we strive to make a positive impact on the world.  Our development programs help support your growth, shaping your future and pushing the boundaries of innovation to unleash potential. Job's mission As a key member of our Global Supplier Development team, this role supports the organization's objective to strengthen supplier capability, elevate design and manufacturing performance, and ensure long-term supply resilience across our global network. The mission of the team is to proactively build supplier maturity, drive continuous improvement, and align supplier capability growth with mid- and long-term category strategies to support product quality, operational excellence, and New Product Introduction (NPI) readiness. What you will be working on Assess supplier quality and technology gaps, and execute plans to address them. Review and influence Design Engineering early in design phase to achieve target costs and DFx. Identify areas for cost down during design phase plus manufacturing methods and eliminate waste. Evaluate supplier processes and risks; collaborate to improve capabilities through lean supplier development methods (i.e. six sigma) and implement mitigation plans. Apply engineering and material science expertise with suppliers to boost capacity, product quality, yield and reduce costs. Partner with Category leaders to drive long-term category strategies to support aggressive production scaling. Align development efforts with long-term category strategies and develop partners within the suppliers. Conduct on-site supplier assessments (capabilities, MSA, PCS/SPCE, etc) and identify gaps during selection. Implement improvement plans with suppliers to ensure readiness and support NPI. Coordinate globally with Business/Key Product Unit Engineering teams to meet end-use specifications. Advance manufacturing capabilities, DFMEA / FMEA, and implement Process Control Systems, PFMEA, at key sites. Support statistical PCS / SPC (lean six sigma) data analysis for internal and external stakeholders. Perform root cause failure analysis and report findings to relevant teams. Lead supplier engagement, support surveillance audits, and qualification activities globally. Independently manage supplier improvement projects, including onboarding training and qualification. Oversee multiple projects to resolve issues and enhance product performance, reliability, and efficiency. Lead / mentor group of Supplier Development team on best practices and develop skillsets to support group growth and expansion What we are looking for MS, or PhD in Engineering, Science, or related technical field (Engineering, Physics, Material Sciences) relevant to semiconductor equipment, or proven expertise. At least 10 years of experience or significant expertise in engineering, designing, and manufacturing of complex mechanical systems. Experience in the semiconductor capital equipment industry with technical background in semiconductor manufacturing technologies required. Strong English communication skills for complex technical topics. Effective collaboration across regions, cultures, and disciplines. Proven Lead Engineer in matrix environments and ability to work independently while managing a team. Proficient in project and time management skills required Drive business process efficiencies and improvements with key internal stakeholders Candidate should have knowledge of the following tools, but will be trained to more advanced levels in: Applied Operational and Engineering Statistical Methods Process Control Systems (PCS) and Statistical Process Control (SPC) FMEA include Design FMEA (DFMEA) and Process FMEA (PFMEA) Structured Problem Solving (8D) and Effective Problem Solving (EPS) Operational Excellence tools including Failure Analysis, Lean Manufacturing, Value Stream Mapping Project Management, Team Management, Escalation Management and Communication Techniques across a broad range of business escalation types, (Customer, Supplier, Technical, Manufacturing, etc.) Should costing estimate modeling (aPriori) Apply today to be part of what’s next. We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn , Facebook , Instagram, X and YouTube . ASM is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, nationality, social or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender, gender identify or expression, marital status, pregnancy, political affiliation, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Quantitative Researcher (UX and Website Analytics)
description: We are looking for a Quantitative Researcher (UX and Website Analytics) to help uncover meaningful user insights that drive product and experience decisions. In this role, you will work closely with Product, Design, Engineering, and Business teams to understand user needs, validate solutions, and shape digital experiences through evidence-based research. You will lead and execute both qualitative and quantitative research, translate insights into actionable recommendations, and support product teams throughout the full product lifecycle. Key Responsibilities 🔍 Research & Insights Plan and conduct qualitative and quantitative research including: User interviews Surveys Usability testing (web & mobile) A/B testing Card sorting and concept testing Synthesise research findings into clear, actionable insights. Combine research data with analytics, CSAT, and behavioural metrics to inform decisions. 📊 Strategy & Analysis Apply mixed-methods research to create a holistic view of user behaviour. Support product strategy with evidence-based insights. Identify trends, pain points, and opportunities across multiple products. 🤝 Stakeholder Collaboration Work closely with Product Managers, Designers, Engineers, and Business stakeholders. Present research findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences. Participate in design workshops, ideation sessions, and product reviews. 📑 Communication & Documentation Produce research reports, insights decks, journey maps, and documentation. Translate insights into practical design and business recommendations. Requirements Minimum 4 years of experience in UX Research or related roles. Strong experience in both qualitative and quantitative research methods . Proven ability to synthesise insights from surveys, analytics, and usability studies. Solid understanding of UX principles, service design, and user-centred design. Experience creating: Journey maps Service blueprints Process flows Ability to work cross-functionally with product, design, engineering, and business teams. Strong communication and storytelling skills. Familiarity with design and prototyping tools (e.g., Figma, Adobe Creative Suite). Comfortable working in fast-paced, agile environments. Good to Have Degree or certification in UX, HCI, Service Design, or related fields. Experience with advanced quantitative research methods. Exposure to large-scale digital platforms or product ecosystems. Experience working in agile or product-led organisations. To apply,simply click the "Apply" button or send your updated profile to recruit@percept-solutions.com EA Licence No.:18S9405 / EA Reg. No.:R1330864 PerceptSolutions is expanding and actively seeking talented individuals. title: Staff Product Engineer
description: About the job At UMC, your career is built on solid ground. Through exposure to the world’s leading semiconductor technologies, guided mentorship, and a culture built on growth and collaboration, we help you forge a strong foundation for your future. With the expansion of our new Fab in Singapore, you’ll be part of a next-generation engineering hub, designed to empower local talent and lead semiconductor innovation in the region. With us, you can expect at UMC : Structured Development : Hands-on training, mentorship, and clear pathways to build your core skills. Front-Row Tech Access : Work with cutting-edge tools that push industries forward. Heart-Fuelled Innovation : Thrive in a team-first environment that values your growth and purpose. Main Responsibilities As a Product Engineer in our team, you will: Conduct New products IP/Device/layout analysis to evaluate production risk Customer products and UMC self IP robustness development to make sure product level qualification pass. Set up FA methodology of new production products, failure analysis cycle time control to speed up production progress. Manage customer products and Improve yield to meet customer requirement. Investigate product low yield root cause by CP program debug, datalog analysis, CP/WAT/in line correlation analysis, FA, reliability assessment and co-work with FAB to resolve the problem in time. You’ll play a hands-on role in building foundational technologies that power everyday innovation, from smart devices to sustainable solutions. We’re looking for someone with: A bachelor’s degree in Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Optoelectronic Engineering, Chemical Engineering or related field At least 5 years of relevant experience in semiconductor/foundry environment is preferred but not mandatory. Strong analytical thinking with effective communication and time management skills. Demonstrated leadership through school projects, competitions, or similar experiences is advantageous. Willingness to work shift hours as required. Ready to Precision-Engineer Your Future? Whether you’re looking for your first role or your next career move, UMC is where your future can take shape. Step into your future with us. Apply Now. About United Microelectronics Corporation United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) is a leading global semiconductor foundry with over 40 years of innovation powering technologies across AI, automotive, consumer electronics, connectivity, and IoT. As one of the industry’s most established players, we enable breakthrough technologies through precision-engineered semiconductor manufacturing for customers across every major sector of the electronics world. Headquartered in Taiwan, UMC has been present in Singapore since 2004 and continues to invest in local talent, technology, and advanced manufacturing. Our recent Fab expansion reflects our long-term commitment to building Singapore as a key hub for semiconductor innovation, and to creating stable, meaningful careers through innovation, teamwork, and progress. Join a team that invests in your future from day one. With best-in-class training, hands-on experience and access to cutting-edge tools, UMC equips you to lead tomorrow’s tech landscape, right down to the nanometre.

title: Senior Production Engineer

Others: Senior Production Engineer may be delegated with additional duties by the reporting heads. However, such delegation does not relieve his or her overall work responsibilities as stated above. Preferred Experience: 3 - 6 years in Aerospace industry in supervisory positions relating to operations. 3 - 6 years of chemical processing experience including but not limited to anodizing, electroplating, chemical cleaning, etc.

title: Senior/ Physiotherapist
description: The Senior/ Physiotherapist develops and provides rehabitative care and services based on the care model, to residents of the Nursing Home(s). You may be involved in developing prevention and treatment programmes with inputs from the residents and may use advanced technology or robotics to relieve pain, improve muscle strength, joint range and mobility, increase exercise tolerance, and improve respiratory function. You will also provide caregiver education to facilitate residents’ recovery. Location: Bukit Batok/Senja/Woodlands/ Tampines Care Home Clinical Care Assess, evaluate and identify rehabilitation needs of residents; formulate/develop and implement appropriate individualised physiotherapy programmes to facilitate, restore, improve or maintain movement/function. Evaluate, monitor and review treatment on a regular basis to document needs and progress of diets and ensure that treatment is appropriate and effective. Evaluate and prescribe assistive devices to improve function in activities of daily living as required by residents. Maintain good communication and liaise with various disciplines involved in the rehabilitation process and refer residents to respective professionals for consultation and appropriate intervention. Training & Development Guide, supervise and train physiotherapy students, therapist assistants, caregivers and volunteers on their performance of duties and implementation of programmes, to ensure safe and effective treatment of residents. Provide training for caregivers in preparation for residents' discharge back into the home environment. Administration Maintain database/records of all residents to provide evidence-based treatment with accurate and appropriate documentation. Ensure that proper documentation is made for all treatment rendered to residents. Any other duty as assigned by the Rehab Manager or Director of Nursing Requirements : Diploma/ Degree in physiotherapy recognised by the Singapore Allied Health Professional Council. Preferably with 1 to 2 years of experience, with experience in rehab services and hospitals, care homes and rehab centres. Fully Registered Therapist with the Singapore Allied Health Professional Council preferred We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: System Engineer
description: We are searching for a System Engineer to be part of our diverse team here in Glamco. Our System Engineers are key component of our operations by providing technical support, testing, installation, training, support and maintenance services for our programs in Singapore. In addition, the System Engineer shall support the Program / Project Manager on the project and system maintenance life cycle from pre-sales to maintenance. Candidates must show initiative, highly independent, hands-on, aspiration for increase role and responsibilities, and a demonstrated willingness to work in a team. Job Responsibilities: Reporting to the Program / Project Manager, the System Engineer will be using their expertise and experience to support both internal Glamco and external Customer activities for our Singapore commercial program. Specific responsibilities but not limited to the following includes: · Provide system engineering and networking support to our programs in Singapore including but not limited to hardware and software configuration and set up, troubleshooting networks and system defects, and fielding technical questions about hardware and software. · Manage and provide onsite and remote customer support including system and infrastructure installation, configuration, testing, training, maintenance, site-surveys etc. · Provide system engineering and networking expertise to our programs in Singapore during pre-sales and post-sales. · Develop, prepare and conduct test cases for customer’s program. · Develop and maintain technical and configuration documentation, training materials and user manuals. · Serve as a Technical Support expert and capable of handling escalations, incidents, outages and infrastructure challenges. · Evaluate and upgrade system hardware and/or firmware with consultation with program partners. Perform system patching and deployment. · Lead and manage a team of Technical Officers to provide technical and maintenance support to clients. · Support the Program / Project Manager to manage the project and system maintenance lifecycle from Proposal development to Project Kickoff to Initial Design to Development and Testing to Customer Acceptance Testing to System Maintenance Program. · Support the Business Development Team in determining project scope of work. · Ensure project and maintenance SLA (Service Level Agreement) and customer satisfaction levels are met. · Maintain standard operating procedures (SOP), processes and guidelines. · Maintain security, backup and redundancy strategies. · Research new technologies and solution in support of the programs. · Any other technical, operations or project related duties that may be required. Requirements · Diploma or Degree in Information Technology or Computer Engineering or related field. · Minimum of 3 years working experience in IT Support, Network, Systems, Cyber Security type function. · Knowledge or experience with network and system administration including firewall and network infrastructure topology, networking protocols, routing and switching, and firewall configuration. · Knowledge or experience with server and workstation administration. · Checkpoint Firewalls, PaloAlto Firewalls, Fortinet Firewall, Cisco Switches, HP Aruba Switches, VPN Technologies, DNS, NTP, 2 Factor Authentication technologies. · Experience in running projects is a plus points. · Strong interest and experience in the field of Radar and Radio Frequency (RF) technologies. · Hardware, software and technical aptitude. · Strong analytical and critical thinking skills. · Show initiative, self-discipline, excellent judgment, career aspirations for increased role and responsibilities, a demonstrated willingness to work as a team with the ability to work independently. · Willing to perform potential shift duties, weekends and overtime. · Willing to travel overseas to support overseas requirements. · Willing to work in an SME environment. · Must possess Class 3 Manual driving license. - Ownership of a car is an advance. About Glamco: GLAMCO Aviation Pte Ltd (GLAMCO) prides itself as a leading solutions provider for the Aerospace, Commercial, Air & Maritime Traffic Management and Defence & Security industries. Established in Singapore since 1985, GLAMCO provides component repair and overhaul, spares support, specialized products and other related consultancy services to Aircraft operators, Defence and Security agencies, Civil Airport and Seaport Operators. Representing Aircraft Manufacturers, Systems OEMs, Stocking Distributors and OEMs worldwide, we are able to support Programme Management, quick response and delivery. GLAMCO has also expanded its activities to include the supply of specialized equipment for the Navy, Army, as well as Civil Airport and Seaport. Since 2020s, GLAMCO has further expanded our services to provide logistics management (warehouse), system engineering and maintenance support to our customers. To learn more about us and our products, please visit our website at www.glamco.com.sg .

title: Scientist II (Molecular Diagnostics), DxD Hub
description: Position Overview Seeking a highly motivated scientist to join the Molecular Diagnostics team, focused on developing regulated clinical diagnostic products and services, especially in nucleic acid-based testing, such as but not limited to molecular approaches like next-generation sequencing (NGS)-based diagnostic assays and nucleic acid  amplification-based diagnostic assays. The successful candidate will lead the development of innovative workflows for clinical applications and work closely with clinical and commercial stakeholders to deliver successful and impactful solutions. This position additionally includes project management responsibilities across all phases of assay and product development. Key Responsibilities Contribute to project scoping and preparing project proposals with in-depth understanding in clinical workflow, clinical unmet needs, technology value proposition, economic value capture, clinical deployment, and product commercialization pathway. Contribute to molecular test assay method development, analytical verification and clinical validation study design, planning and execution in compliance with regulatory and quality standards. Contribute to the study results data curation and data analysis for downstream molecular test assay application. Lead project teams to ensure the successful delivery of molecular diagnostics development milestones, leveraging effective project planning, resource management, and risk mitigation strategies. Establish, manage, and monitor project plans, timelines, budgets, and deliverables, ensuring alignment with regulatory requirements, quality standards, and company objectives. Facilitate regular project meetings, document minutes and action items, and communicate progress transparently to internal and external stakeholders. Identify and resolve project bottlenecks, technical risks, and resource gaps, escalating issues as needed to management. Contribute to clinical study design and clinical trial in regulated diagnostic product settings. Contribute to product landscape study on safety dossier and technical note approved by regulators. Responsible for mentoring junior scientists and refresher on the latest technology development and product landscape. Manage and perform lab operations activities, including general laboratory and equipment housekeeping and qualifications, and consumables purchasing. Responsible for good record-keeping and standard operating procedures (SOPs) implementation in accordance with confidential information requirement, quality system and regulatory guidelines. Requirements Education Education in Molecular Biology, Cell Biology, Biochemistry, Genomics, Biotechnology, or a related field. Experience 3-5 years of hands-on experience in molecular assay development, preferably in industrial, diagnostics or clinical laboratory setting using platforms such as NGS or PCR/qPCR technologies. Laboratory skills in pre-analytical and analytical techniques, including extensive knowledge and hands-on experience in molecular techniques and platforms including, but not limited to, nucleic acid extraction, PCR/qPCR, NGS, isothermal amplification and CRISPR-based diagnostic tools. Strong scientific foundation, demonstrated critical thinking abilities, and proven experience applying first-principles methodology. Strong track record in experimental design, troubleshooting, and optimisation of laboratory workflows. Project management experience in a laboratory, clinical or diagnostic product development context is strongly preferred. Familiarity with regulatory and quality standards for diagnostic assay development e.g. ISO13485 is advantageous. Other Attributes A strong team player with a proactive and upbeat attitude. Solid academic knowledge and a critical and innovative mindset. Strong in identifying critical pathways and demonstrate problem-solving abilities. Strong and effective communication talents in complex and changing environment. Fast in the response and precise in articulation. Great presentation skills for a mixed audience. Optimistic and enthusiastic about continuous learning and professional development in the rapidly evolving field. Extensive industrial relation and understanding of product landscapes is advantageous. The above eligibility criteria are not exhaustive. title: Low‑Code Developer (Mendix)
description: Mendix Low‑Code Engineer — Build Enterprise‑Scale Impact at Kyndryl If you're looking for a role where your low‑code expertise genuinely shapes the way large organizations operate, this is the place for you. At Kyndryl, you won’t just build small apps—you’ll design enterprise‑grade Mendix solutions that thousands of users rely on daily. You’ll join a global engineering culture where your ideas matter, your skills are trusted, and your work directly improves how businesses run. Most companies use low‑code for basic prototypes or internal utilities. At Kyndryl, you’ll use Mendix to modernize mission‑critical systems and transform complex processes at scale. You’ll own the full solution lifecycle, work with enterprise architects, and influence how entire organizations adopt digital transformation. What You’ll Do You’ll play a key role in designing intelligent, scalable Mendix applications that streamline operations and deliver real business outcomes. Your work will be hands‑on, strategic, and highly visible—giving you the autonomy to build solutions that truly make an impact. 1) Design, Build & Deploy Enterprise Mendix Applications You’ll create end‑to‑end applications using Mendix Studio Pro—including domain models, microflows, UI pages, reusable components, and secure user roles. You’ll build applications that reduce manual work, improve decision‑making, and solve real operational challenges. 2) Streamline & Automate Business Processes You’ll use Mendix workflows, logic flows, rules, and automation capabilities to eliminate inefficiencies. You’ll turn manual steps into intelligent workflows, integrate data across systems, and ensure everything runs smoothly with proper validation and governance. 3) Integrate Systems & Build Scalable Data Models You’ll design clean, scalable data models and connect Mendix apps with enterprise systems—via REST/SOAP APIs, Databases, Microsoft services, or ERP/CRM platforms. Your integrations will form the backbone of seamless cross‑system operations. Why You’ll Love Working at Kyndryl You’ll build for the enterprise, not just for a department. Your applications will support real business operations—governments, banks, global supply chains, healthcare institutions, and more. You get ownership. Real ownership. You’ll influence solution design, architecture decisions, and integration patterns—not just “configure forms” or “drag and drop screens.” You grow into the next version of yourself. Kyndryl invests heavily in talent development. As you expand your Mendix capability, you’ll have clear paths toward roles such as: Low‑Code Solution Architect Technical Lead (Mendix) Enterprise Integration Engineer Platform Owner / Low‑Code Governance Lead Full Stack or Hybrid Developer (if that’s a path you want) Here, you’re not limited to low‑code—you can grow your engineering, cloud, architecture, and leadership career in whichever direction you choose. Required Skills & Experience At least 4 years of hands‑on Mendix or low‑code development experience (Mendix Studio Pro strongly preferred). Experience working with Git and Agile teams (Scrum, CI, pair programming). Knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL/NoSQL databases. Experience integrating apps with REST/SOAP APIs and enterprise systems. Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, plus the ability to collaborate with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders. High attention to detail and commitment to delivering quality applications. Preferred Skills & Experience Mendix certifications (Rapid, Intermediate, Advanced, Expert). Experience with CI/CD pipelines or DevOps practices. Exposure to mobile development in Mendix. Familiarity with other low‑code platforms (OutSystems, Power Apps, Appian, ServiceNow, Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online). Experience managing platform‑level features such as rules engines, workflows, access roles, validations, UI flows, and reusable modules. At Kyndryl, you’ll be part of a global engineering powerhouse where low‑code isn’t an afterthought—it’s a strategic capability. You’ll design impactful applications, solve meaningful problems, and grow into a technical leader who shapes how enterprises operate. If you're ready to build solutions that matter, this is where your next chapter begins.

title: Principal Engineer, NAND TD, RDA
description: Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all . Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever. As a Principal Engineer in NAND TD RDA , you will lead a team in the use of next-generation defect detection equipment and processes to identify and solve issues. The goal of this position is to accelerate cycles of learning and technology transfer in Technology Development. We need leaders who are passionate about developing their team, delivering solutions to our internal customers, and driving our supplier's road maps to meet our future inspection needs. This position will provide the opportunity for you to collaborate with process integration, process development, data science, and yield analysis within and outside Technology Development. As a Technology Development RDA Lead, your leadership and innovation will have the ability to impact the team globally. You and your team will be responsible to support experimentation, data analysis, baseline health metrics, and systematic and random defect improvement through partnering with others in Technical Development and Global Manufacturing. Responsibility includes , but not limited to: Encourage a safe work environment Mentor your team in analyzing experimental process flows looking for defect anomalies and quarantining process tools that are out of control Build an environment of continuous improvement process (CIP) demeanor, looking for ways to improve operations, and how to make a difference today Develop inspection technology roadmaps to drive our internal and external partners enabling a clear path to future advanced memory needs Manage projects internal to your team and across functional areas Direct your team to recognize, identify, and raise awareness of process anomalies Develop clear metrics for program health and communicate in an effective manor to set program priorities Build and improve upon detection techniques and identify new ways to use existing tools to identify relevant process issues Active participation in technical meetings and subsequently, synthesize new opportunities or novel approaches from the same Collaborate with the global network to develop best known methods and maintain alignment between sites Proficiency in data analysis, machine learning, and statistical modeling to derive actionable insights from complex datasets is an added advantage Requirements : PhD /Masters in Applied Mechanics, Mechanical Engineering, Microelectronics or Electrical and Electronics Engineering 6-8 years RDA experience in the semiconductor industry working in the process development for Equipment/Applications 6 - 8 years of experience in RDA Defect, semiconductor optical and electron beam inspection methods and e-beam technologies Exposure in various tools like KLA, AMAT Proactive and passionate about learning and mentoring your team, and a leader drawn to creative problem solving and innovative solutions, will investigate process anomalies, root cause analysis, data manipulation, and correlations Proven track record in innovating and drive effective change in an energizing environment Has shown combination of flexibility and tenacity in the pursuit of goals Excellent technical, communication, and analytical skills Develops new or modified process formulations, defines processing or handling equipment requirements and specifications, and reviews processing techniques and methods applied in the manufacture, fabrication, and evaluation of products. Involvement may begin at any step from pilot plant to full-scale manufacturing. Coordinates design requirement review with appropriate engineering/scientific personnel to ensure compatibility of processing methods. Compiles and evaluates test data to determine appropriate limits and variables for process or material specifications. May conceive and plan projects involving definition and selection of new concepts, equipment automation technology, and approaches in the processing or development of new or improved processes.

title: Research Associate (Singapore Literature)
description: The English Programme at Nanyang Technological University hosts a dynamic community of students and professors engaged in a wide variety of literary passions and projects. It is a research-intensive department with a particular focus in five key research areas: Singapore Literature, Modern Literature, Contemporary Literature and Culture, Period Studies, and Irish Studies. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/soh/about-us/english . We are looking for a Research Associate to provide research and administrative support for an MOE Tier 1-funded project, “Smart Nation: A Literary Atlas”. The project is a scholarly intervention that sets out to provide a fresh perspective on the complex negotiations between state, society, and science and technology in Singapore in order to address the vital and urgent questions pertaining to the necessary re-evaluation of these relationships in the twenty-first century and the role that culture plays in this task. Key outputs include a monograph on cultural representations of science and technology in Singaporean literature and a seminar series featuring prominent researchers working at the intersection of science and technology and literature. Key Responsibilities: Collate annotated bibliography of Singaporean literary works that engage with science and technology as well as related themes Close read Singaporean literary works that engage with science and technology as well as related themes Assist with archival research, both online and on-site Assist with the preparation of manuscripts Assist with administrative tasks pertaining to the organization of a seminar series Oversee a small team of undergraduate student research assistants Job Requirements: Master qualification degree in English Literature or related field Good written and oral communication skills Ability to work independently under strict timelines with an eye for detail Familiarity with Singaporean literature and/or literature engaging with science and technology-related issues is an advantage. Entry level candidates are welcome to apply. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

The Building Services team are a source of world leading expertise – and offer a pro-active and collaborative approach to skill development, and professional excellence. This is a key position necessary to lead the wet lab section of the newly setup Genomic Diagnostics Lab (GDL) to support NUH’s newly setup National University Centre for Genomic Medicine (NUGEM) and the National Precision Medicine’s Initiatives. These include but are not limited to Sample Processing, Library Preparation & Sequencing processes, Validation & Verification of Test Protocols. Apply a thorough understanding of scientific principles in service delivery, with a focus on troubleshooting and optimizing laboratory assays. Apply a thorough understanding of bioinformatics tools for primary data analysis, Primers & Probes design and SNP-ID analysis. Supervise the laboratory’s participation in external quality control programmes to maintain compliance with clinical and regulatory standards. Lead, mentor and train Medical Laboratory Technologists/Scientists for wet lab and bioinformatics processes. Work closely with a multidisciplinary team, including clinicians, medical laboratory scientists, operations and administrative personnel, to drive integrated service delivery. Participate in the management process within the laboratory, including human resource management, staff development and strategic planning. Perform any other duties as assigned by reporting officer. Job Requirements Good Class Honours’ Degree/BSc/MSc/PH.D. in genetics, genomics, human genetics, or a related field within the biological sciences or life science discipline. Minimum 10 years of experience in wet-lab processing and methodological verifications. Hands on experience with strong technical expertise in molecular biology and NGS workflows. Knowledge and experience of quality management systems, including compliance with ISO15189 and/or CAP standards or equivalent Proficiency in bioinformatics tools and data interpretation. Meticulous attention to detail and commitment to quality assurance. Ability to troubleshoot and optimize protocols independently. Effective communication and documentation skills for regulatory compliance. Minimum 3 years in a leadership or supervisory position, demonstrating capabilities in leading a team. Positive attitude, proactive mindset and a willingness to take initiatives. High level of attention to details with strong organization skills and ability to work under tight timeline.

Support student progress across the portfolio, provide guidance on academic and career pathways relevant to CET, and implement strategies to enhance student retention and success, including recognition of prior learning (RPL) where applicable. Develop and manage the overall budget for the assigned portfolio, ensuring resources are allocated efficiently to support program goals and collaborative CET/IHL activities. Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with industry partners, SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG), other CET providers, and relevant departments within other IHLs to ensure the portfolio's relevance, create opportunities for student upskilling and reskilling, and explore potential collaborations across different programs. Supporting learners in their career placement pathway . Creating Recruitment fair for industry partners and learners to network and create placement event. Sales & Enrollment Growth (Portfolio & CET/IHL Engagement): Develop and implement strategic enrollment plans for the portfolio of courses and programs, with a specific focus on attracting working professionals, career switchers, and individuals seeking CET qualifications for various offerings. Represent the institute's CET-related programs at recruitment events, industry seminars, and collaborative initiatives with other IHLs and CET providers. Collaborate closely with the marketing and admissions teams to develop targeted marketing materials, website content, and digital campaigns that resonate with the CET audience for the diverse program offerings and highlight their alignment with national skills frameworks. Engage with prospective CET learners and their employers across the portfolio, providing detailed information about the various program curricula, career advancement opportunities, and the benefits of utilizing SkillsFuture credits and other CET funding schemes. Conduct presentations and workshops for prospective CET students and corporate clients across the portfolio, emphasizing the relevance of different programs to industry needs and their articulation with other IHL qualifications where applicable. Track and analyze enrollment data across the portfolio, including the profile of CET learners in different programs, to evaluate the effectiveness of recruitment strategies and partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. Build and maintain relationships with alumni from various programs who have leveraged their education for career progression within the CET framework. Explore and implement innovative recruitment strategies tailored to the CET audience for the diverse portfolio, including partnerships with employers, online learning platforms, and collaborations with other training providers and IHLs. Minimum of 2 years of experience in academic administration or program management within a higher education institution, with demonstrable experience in or strong understanding of the CET sector in Singapore. Demonstrated experience in developing curriculum relevant to industry needs and aligned with CET frameworks (e.g., modularization, stackability) across multiple programs. Proven track record of successful student recruitment, particularly within the adult learning or CET market, for a range of educational offerings. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse audiences, including industry professionals and CET stakeholders, across different program areas. Strong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and initiatives concurrently and a collaborative mindset. Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment and across different institutions and organizations. Familiarity with the higher education and CET landscape in Singapore, including SkillsFuture initiatives and relevant funding schemes. Experience in developing and managing partnerships with industry and/or CET providers across a portfolio of programs is highly desirable. Experience with CRM systems and enrollment management software, as well as platforms relevant to CET learners, is an advantage. Personal Attributes: Passionate about lifelong learning and empowering working professionals through a diverse range of educational opportunities. Highly motivated and results-oriented with a proactive approach to building partnerships and driving enrollment within the CET sector for a portfolio of programs. Strong understanding of the needs and motivations of adult learners across different fields. Excellent networking and stakeholder management skills. Strong ethical standards and a commitment to academic integrity and the principles of continuing education. Adaptable and resilient in a dynamic environment, with a willingness to engage with the evolving CET landscape across various program areas. A strong advocate for the institute and its diverse program offerings within both the IHL and CET communities.

title: Research Engineer (in Multimodal AI ) - MRP
description: Job Purpose: The primary responsibility of this role is to deliver on an industry innovation research project where you will be part of the research team to develop a Multimodal AI for Fire Detection & Safety Systems. Key Responsibilities: Participate in and manage the research project with Principal Investigator (PI), Collaborator and the research team members to ensure all project deliverables are met. Undertake these responsibilities in the project: - Develop and deploy multimodal AI algorithms for fire, smoke, and hot-work detection by fusing optical, thermal/infrared, LiDAR, RADAR, and gas sensor data under varying environmental conditions. - Design computer vision and human-behavior analysis models for detecting personnel, posture, casualties, and hazardous situations, including operation in low-visibility scenarios. - Implement scene mapping, environment perception, and path-planning algorithms using LiDAR and RADAR data to support evacuation guidance and first-responder navigation. - Build real-time predictive models for fire intensity and spread forecasting and integrate LLM-based scene description and decision support into an end-to-end deployable system. Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations. Coordinate procurement and liaison with vendors/suppliers. Work independently, as well as within a team, to ensure proper operation and maintenance of equipment. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree (minimum) in Electrical / Electronics Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Robotics, or a closely related discipline, with foundational knowledge in signal processing and machine learning. Working knowledge of computer vision and deep learning concepts, including object detection and image-based classification, with hands-on experience using Python and at least one deep learning framework (e.g., PyTorch or TensorFlow). Basic experience handling and processing sensor data (e.g., camera, thermal, LiDAR, RADAR, or similar perception sensors) in real-world or laboratory settings. Familiarity with Linux-based development environments and standard software tools for data analysis, model training, and evaluation. Ability to independently implement, test, and document applied research solutions, with prior experience through projects, internships, or research assistantships considered sufficient. Prior experience in publishing is desirable.

As the member of our team, you will have experience in a similar role and will be given a degree of autonomy and responsibility that suits a self-starting and self-reliant person who can deliver ICT/ELV design and supporting multi-discipline development, such as commercial, residential, hospital, hotel, leisure, mixed-use, critical facilities, data centers, airports, etc. You also will have opportunity to be involved in advisory work, providing advice and high-level consultancy services to clients from various industry. We work as a connected global team, delivering impactful work that is shaping the future across design and delivery, and it is our people who are powering this change. As a proudly employee-owned business, we’re here to benefit our clients, each other and our local and global communities. Our progress enables us to invest in creating the right space for everyone to feel empowered, supported and valued. What you will typically do: Client and stakeholders management and relationships. Communicating with clients, manage clients’ requirements and expectation in alignment with the project’s contractual scope. High-level and conceptual architecting of “state-of-the-art” integrated smart building/precinct systems and infrastructure, incl. client consulting, requirement management, conduct studies, options, gap analysis and solutioning. Design and development of ICT/ELV infrastructure and systems, including spaces and pathways, fiber and copper structured cabling system, communication networking systems, integrated BMS platform, integrated security systems, building systems and data integration. Collaborate on the development of the design package for tendering, incl. functional and performance specification, schedules, drawings, integration and interface management plan, interface coordination, integration and testing plan, etc. Support the tendering process and supervise the implementation on behalf of client. Fluent use of MS Visio, PowerPoint, AutoCAD, Revit, Revu and other equivalent mark-up/engineering tools for drawing, report, and presentation production. About You: You will be a qualified Electrical, Electronics or ICT/Telecommunications Engineer or equivalent who is experienced in the design and specification of ICT/ELV systems and services within buildings. This role ideally suits someone with 4+ years of relevant experience and looking to join a growing team that holds technical excellence to high regard and offer an excellent opportunity for career progression. To be the right person for the role, you will have: Degree in Engineering Current and proven experience as ICT/ELV Engineer with design experience within Buildings, Data Centres, Mission Critical Facilities, Airports, Commercial, Transport sectors. Knowledge and experience in: Building ICT infrastructure (incl. customer-owned Outside Plant) – required Network and communication systems – required Integrated Building Management System – required Building ELV Systems - required ICT/ELV and Building Systems and Data Integration - required Smart Building, IoT, Data Analytics – required Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning – highly desirable Cybersecurity – highly desirable Sustainability - highly desirable ICT/ELV industry certification (e.g. credential from BICSI, WiredScore/SmartScore, EPI, CNET, CompTIA, UTI, Cisco, etc.) – highly desirable Knowledge of Building Industry Standard and regulations Highly developed interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills are essential. title: Resident Physician, Department of Ophthalmology
description: The National University Health System (NUHS) is an integrated Academic Health System and Regional Health System in Singapore that delivers value-driven, innovative and sustainable healthcare. Institutions in the NUHS group include three hospitals – National University Hospital, Ng Teng Fong General Hospital and Jurong Community Hospital; three National Specialty Centres – National University Cancer Institute, Singapore, National University Heart Centre, Singapore and National University Centre for Oral Health, Singapore; a polyclinic group – the National University Polyclinics; one medical centre – Jurong Medical Centre; and three academic health sciences institutions – National University of Singapore (NUS) Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine (including the Alice Lee Centre for Nursing Studies), NUS Faculty of Dentistry and NUS Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health. The Department of Ophthalmology, National University Hospital, is seeking candidates who are highly motivated and willing to join us for a challenging and fulfilling appointment as Resident Physician. As a Resident Physician, you will be responsible for overall supervision and day-to-day running of the clinics (onsite and offsite), including patient care, exercising good diagnostic and clinical management acumen. In addition, you will supervise and teach Medical officers and Primary Care Partners (Family Physicians and General Practitioners), attend and participate in all departmental teaching rounds including the combined Hospital teaching seminars, clinical-pathological case conferences, etc. There will also be opportunities for you to participate in department initiatives (e.g. setting up team-based care), new services (e.g. running a Diagnostic Test Reading Centre) and research projects, where appropriate. • Possess a basic medical degree and a postgraduate (MRCP) which are registrable with the Singapore Medical Council • Possess a 3 year post housemanship clinical experience • Be in active clinical practice • Must be registered with the Singapore Medical Council • Able to demonstrate good diagnostic and clinical management acumen • Able to demonstrate teamwork, respect, integrity, compassion and excellence in his/ her work

title: Technical Engineer
description: About ST Engineering ST Engineering is a global technology, defence, and engineering group with offices across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and the U.S., serving customers in more than 100 countries. The Group uses technology and innovation to solve real-world problems and improve lives through its diverse portfolio of businesses across the aerospace, smart city, defence, and public security segments. Headquartered in Singapore, ST Engineering ranks among the largest companies listed on the Singapore Exchange. Our history spans more than 50 years, and our strategy is underpinned by our core values – Integrity, Value Creation, Courage, Commitment and Compassion. These 5 core values guide every aspect of our business and are embedded in our ST Engineering culture – from the people we hire, to working with each other, to our partners and customers. About our Line of Business – Mission Software & Services Our Mission Software & Services business provides leading-edge mission critical command, control, and communications (C3) systems with secured IT infrastructure and managed services. We support our client’s innovation journey through design thinking, analytics, and AI-enabled decision support with our full suite of cloud computing solutions. We provide intelligent, actionable insights and sustainable solutions to our valued partners in diverse industries including defence, government, and commercial sectors. Together, We Can Make A Significant Impact We are looking for an experienced and proactive Technical Engineer to support the technical development of our Core Platform. The role involves collaborating with vendors, customers, and internal teams to refactor, upgrade, and optimize the platform’s tech stack while also exploring new technologies and AI integrations. The successful candidate will have expertise in modern software development practices, containerization, and platform upgrades. Be Part of Our Success Containerization of Tech Stack: Lead the efforts to containerize the existing tech stack using tools like Docker and Kubernetes, ensuring a scalable and efficient platform. Vendor Coordination & Technical Solutioning: Collaborate with external vendors to design and implement technical solutions that align with business needs and customer requirements. Tech Stack Refactoring: Refactor legacy code and optimize the tech stack, ensuring it meets modern standards, improves performance, and is maintainable. Core & Common Tech Stack Upgrade: Lead the technical upgrade efforts of the core and common tech stack, including evaluating and implementing AI integrations and other cutting-edge technologies. Proof of Concept (PoC): Develop and demonstrate proof of concept (PoC) solutions for new technologies, particularly AI and cloud-based tools, to evaluate their feasibility and impact. Documentation and Reporting: Maintain comprehensive technical documentation, track progress, and report regularly on key milestones and outcomes. Collaboration with Cross-functional Teams: Work closely with product managers, UX/UI teams, and backend developers to ensure smooth integration and delivery of features. Customer Interaction: Engage with customers to gather requirements and provide technical support, ensuring solutions are tailored to meet their needs. Qualities We Value Experience: 3+ years of experience in technical development, with a focus on platform refactoring, upgrades, and containerization. Technical Expertise: Strong experience with containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes). Proficient in JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and React for full-stack development. Experience with serverless architectures. Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and modern DevOps practices. Strong experience with microservices architecture and related technologies (REST APIs, messaging systems like Kafka). Experience in AI integration and working with AI/ML frameworks is a plus. Refactoring skills: Ability to refactor legacy code, improve code quality, and optimize performance. Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Adaptability to work with new technologies and changing requirements. Our Commitment That Goes Beyond the Norm An environment where you will be working on cutting-edge technologies and architectures. Safe space where diverse perspectives are valued, and everyone’s unique contributions are celebrated. Meaningful work and projects that make a difference in people’s lives. A fun, passionate and collaborative workplace. Competitive remuneration and comprehensive benefits.

description: Junior System and Network Administrator (Training & Simulation Projects) Clearance Requirement: Cat2A and above About the Role: We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Junior System and Network Administrator to support the infrastructure behind training simulator projects for defense applications. This entry-level role is ideal for candidates with foundational IT knowledge who are eager to grow their skills in a secure, high-performance simulation environment. You will assist in maintaining systems, supporting project deployments, managing hardware assets, and coordinating with vendors under the guidance of senior staff. Why Join Us? - Gain hands-on experience in defense simulation and training technologies. - Work alongside experienced professionals in a collaborative environment. - Contribute to national defense and mission-critical training initiatives. - Access to mentorship, training, and career development opportunities.

title: Process Safety Engineer
description: We are looking for a Process Safety Engineer (m/f/d) to join our Process Safety department at Advario in Singapore, starting as soon as possible. Job Profile: The Process Safety Engineer leads the implementation and continuous improvement of Process Safety Management (PSM) systems to identify, assess, control and monitor major hazard risks in the terminals. The PSE leads the delivery of Process Safety initiatives by collaborating with Engineering, Operations, HSSE teams and regulatory authorities to ensure compliance with Singapore’s regulations and industry best practices. He/She tracks PSM performance data and contributes to the development and maintenance of the relevant sections of the organisation's Major Hazard Installation (MHI) Safety Case. He/She conducts process safety reviews and hazard/risk studies for existing and new facilities. The PSE also participates in process safety incident investigations and follows up on preventive and corrective actions. Your Tasks: 1) Administer Process Safety Management Systems Support Process Safety framework adoption and implementation for the local organization in accordance to relevant local regulations, internal and industry standards/best practices. Ensure governance of Process Safety Management (PSM) system is in place to achieve organization's operational effectiveness and efficiency, reliable reporting, compliance to laws, regulations and policies through: - Develop operational process safety solutions by defining, studying, estimating, and screening alternative solutions. - Improve operational process safety results by studying, evaluating, and recommending process re-design; implementing changes; contributing information and opinion to engineering design and modification teams. - Monitor and report key process safety performance indicators, track action closure, support audits and compliance reviews, promote continuous improvement in risk control. Investigate Process Safety incidents with the local team and propose suitable improvements / recommendations to avoid reoccurrence. Identifies operational process safety problems by observing and studying system and performance results. 2) Support Process Engineering Projects Review and/or provide support in e.g. Process Hazard Analyses (PHA), MOCs, risk assessments, safety studies, Safety Case and project designs, to ensure process safety related considerations are identified and addressed. Develop recommendations and addressing Process Safety deficiencies identified in these reviews, where applicable and ensure relevant documentation is complete. To systematically seek out process safety improvement opportunities and assess risk to ALARP 3) Administer Staff and Organizational Development Build Process Safety competency in the organization through training sessions on process safety topics. Your Profile: Bachelor’s degree in in Chemical Engineering, Process Engineering, Safety Engineering (or equivalent with relevant experience) Minimum 5 years working experience in oil and gas industry, specializing in process safety and hazard management Strong knowledge of process hazard identification and risk assessment methods and tools (e.g. HAZOP, LOPA, QRA, Bowtie). Knowledge of international and local safety standards and regulatory requirements (e.g., API, NFPA, SS, functional safety). Strong terminal operations knowledge, its drivers from a technical and risk point of view. Good communication, analytical, problem-solving and stakeholder-management skills; able to work across engineering, operations, maintenance and HSSE teams. Proficient in consequence modeling software (preferred). Please note: This position is based in Jurong Island Official Working hours: Mon - Fri, 0830 - 1700 hours We offer you among other things : Special benefits and company events organized for employees Employees are covered under the group insurance plan, with dependent coverage for hospitalization Lunch is provided while in the terminal and company shuttle bus transport to and from Jurong East MRT

Deep hands-on expertise in one or more core technologies such as C#, Java, or modern front-end frameworks; practical experience with AI/ML in production environments is highly desirable. Strong business acumen with experience in aligning technology development with commercial objectives and client needs in global markets. Exceptional communication skills in both English and Chinese, with the ability to articulate vision to technical and non-technical stakeholders. Established thought leadership in Industry 4.0, digital twins, or industrial AI solutions. Experience in managing geographically dispersed R&D or product engineering teams. A history of successful cross-cultural collaboration and global market expansion.

title: Research Fellow (CVMD/JH)
description: Job Description The Duke-NUS Metabolomics Lab is a leading metabolomics core and research facility in Singapore, actively contributing to numerous projects aimed at uncovering disease mechanisms through advanced metabolomics profiling. Write and review research papers, present research outcomes, conceptualise new ideas and develop plans for independent research which could have a considerable influence and impact on the relevant field attracting research funding. Provide support for external grant/protocol development, study design and management of research activities. Proven track record in the form of high impact publications. Strong writing and critical thinking skills, as well as good communication and presentation skills. title: Principal Electronics Engineer
description: Position Overview: The Principal Electronics Engineer is a senior leadership role within EOS Defence Systems (EOS DS) and is responsible for solving problems using disciplined engineering techniques (e.g., software, mechanical, electronics, system, testing, project etc) to assure and advise on the development and/or delivery of products. The Principal Electronics Engineer possess deep technical and industry knowledge with the ability to influence and guide company direction, ensuring strategic alignment. The Principal Electronics Engineer is also responsible for developing products and designs that support the product development and product goals of EOS DS. The Principal Electronics Engineer will lead and mentor engineers, up to and including Senior Engineers. They may provide technical assistance to multiple teams and provide a high level of support to those teams. In consultation with senior leadership, suggest staffing and workflow priorities and monitor and manage performance, health and wellbeing. The Principal Electronics Engineer will have extensive engineering experience across the product lifecycle in a Defence environment, allowing them to provide leadership across engineering teams and execute technical authority as allocated. Primary Duties: Specific Functions & Tasks Provide necessary guidance and direction to produce electronics designs and analysis that satisfy defined product development requirements in the specified time frames and exercise allocated technical authority. Work with systems engineers to ensure all requirements are identified, captured and analysed. Lead conceptual design and schematic capture of complex electronic circuits. Ensure test and evaluation activities on electronics and system designs are undertaken to verify and sign-off of all design requirements. Initiate and/or participate in planning on electronics designs. Develop, review and champion the electronics engineering process within EOS DS. Ensure the preparation and review of all documentation to support designs. Perform conceptual, preliminary and detailed design of complex electronics assemblies. Simulate analogue and digital circuits. Document designs including calculations, design options and decisions. Prepare for and contribute to design reviews. Perform test, evaluation, characterisation, verification & validation of electronic subsystems in collaboration with test engineers. Prototype electronic circuits, wiring harnesses and other systems. Work closely with other engineering disciplines to ensure cohesive and complete electronic designs are delivered on time and on budget and meet project requirements. Apply DFx principles (manufacturability, testability, excellence). Review work and provide guidance and mentorship to Senior Electronics Engineers. Engage key stakeholders and subject matter experts across technical engineering and specialty areas to understand the concept of operations and user needs to elicit and decompose electrical requirements. Adhere to and ensure understanding and compliance with company procedures to achieve a safe and secure work environment. Other duties as required. Strategy and Architecture Contribute to and influence strategy for emerging, new, and current technologies, products, services within Defence Systems and have authority over and accountable for the innovation and delivery practices required to assure the implementation of this strategy. Assess and ensure a high level of leadership and deep industry, defence system knowledge and systems engineering best practice to maintain competitive advantage, exploit EOS DS technologies and minimise exposure and risk. Communicate, advocate and influence persuasively through effective negotiation and communication while fostering an environment of innovation, learning and continuous improvement within and outside the organisation. Development and implementation of Engineering Self-initiated and managed under broad direction and governance to be responsible for demonstrably leading self and others to produce designs and products that satisfy defined requirements in the specified time frames and meet group objectives. Undertake cohesive best practice designs and analysis and develop efficient work practices to support product development activities and business outcomes. Lead and work collaboratively with engineers across functional teams to solve complex problems as a team and ensure all requirements are identified, captured, and analysed such that the design/product can be developed that satisfies the safety, quality, policy, legislative and design authority requirements. Undertake clear and pragmatic tests, evaluation, and verification activities on integrated system designs and prototypes to verify and provide recommendations for sign-off of all design/product requirements to the Chief Engineer and Design Authorities based on relevant legislative requirements, standards, and contracts. Prepare and review all documentation required to support designs and provide tailored advice and recommendations to teams and decision makers. Relationship and engagement Actively listen, share knowledge and experience, provide guidance and mentorship, and work collaboratively to mobilise teams across engineering and other relevant internal and external customers to deliver engineering and business outcomes. Work with Production Engineers and Technicians to ensure the best design solutions are delivered to solve production problems and obtain business outcomes under the broad direction of the Chief Engineer. Support the leadership alongside with the EOS Executive, Project Managers, and Project Engineers to ensure design solutions are delivered on time and on budget and meet other project management requirements. Communicate insights and provide thorough engineering judgement for progress and the advancement of engineering solutions. Apply interdisciplinary, collaborative approach to requirements including analysis of functional and non-functional requirements and functional performance specifications to develop effective solutions. Delivery and operation Guide the design and execution of user acceptance testing and other verification activities to provide recommendations to the Chief Engineer and other Design Authorities. Support production and review manufacturing and supplier requirements to meet specified timeframes, engineering standards, and business outcomes. People and skills Work in cross functional multidisciplinary teams to enable self and others to solve engineering problems and implement solutions. Key Attributes: Ethical Leader. Influencer. Deep Technical knowledge. Problem solver. Engineering judgement. Details oriented. Works Collaboratively. Insightful. Communicates well. Inventive. Solution orientated. Team builder Uses experience for sound judgement
